Then, \(f\) is right-cancellable and is an epimorphism. ■







5.1.1.4 Proposition: In \(\mathsf{Top}\) the empty space \(\emptyset\) (Example 3.1.3.1) is the initial object and the point spaces (as representatives of) \(*\) (Example 3.1.3.2) are the terminal objects.


[点击展开翻译]

5.1.1.4 命题： 在 \(\mathsf{Top}\) 中，空空间 \(\emptyset\)（例 3.1.3.1）是始对象，而点空间（作为其代表的）\(*\)（例 3.1.3.2）是终对象。










5.1.1.5 Proposition: In \(\mathsf{Top}\) the unique morphism from \(\emptyset\) to a topological space \(X\) is the empty map; the initial topology on the set \(X\) induced by this map is the trivial topology. The unique morphism from a topological space \(X\) to (any representative of) \(*\) is the constant map; the final topology on the set \(X\) induced by this map is the discrete topology.


[点击展开翻译]

5.1.1.5 命题： 在 \(\mathsf{Top}\) 中，从 \(\emptyset\) 到拓扑空间 \(X\) 的唯一态射是空映射；由该映射诱导的集合 \(X\) 上的初始拓扑是平凡拓扑。从拓扑空间 \(X\) 到（任何代表）\(*\) 的唯一态射是常数映射；由该映射诱导的集合 \(X\) 上的最终拓扑是离散拓扑。












5.1.2.1 The category of small pointed topological spaces: The category \(\mathsf{Top}_*\) is the category of small pointed topological spaces and pointed continuous maps between them as morphisms. The identity takes the identity map at each small pointed topological space, and the composition is the composition of pointed maps.


[点击展开翻译]

5.1.2.1 小带基点拓扑空间范畴： 范畴 \(\mathsf{Top}_*\) 是以小带基点拓扑空间为对象、以它们之间的带基点连续映射为态射的范畴。恒等态射取每个小带基点拓扑空间上的恒等映射，复合运算即为带基点映射的复合。
crumbs: 5. MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES

crumbs: 5. MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES

title: MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES
text:
5.2 Maps between topological spaces.

5.2.1 Open and closed maps.






5.2.1.1 Definition: A map \[f: (X, \mathcal{T}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{T}_Y) \tag{39}\]

is open iff \((\forall U \in \mathcal{T}_X)(f(U) \in \mathcal{T}_Y)\);
is closed iff \((\forall A \in \mathcal{C}_X)(f(A) \in \mathcal{C}_Y)\), where \(\mathcal{C}_X\) and \(\mathcal{C}_Y\) are the collections of closed subsets of \(X\) and \(Y\), respectively.



[点击展开翻译]

5.2.1.1 定义： 一个映射 \(f: (X, \mathcal{T}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{T}_Y)\)：

是开映射，当且仅当 \((\forall U \in \mathcal{T}_X)(f(U) \in \mathcal{T}_Y)\)；
是闭映射，当且仅当 \((\forall A \in \mathcal{C}_X)(f(A) \in \mathcal{C}_Y)\)，其中 \(\mathcal{C}_X\) 和 \(\mathcal{C}_Y\) 分别是 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) 的闭子集族。











5.2.1.2 Theorem: Let \(\mathcal{T}_1\) and \(\mathcal{T}_2\) be two topologies on a set \(X\). Then \(\mathcal{T}_1\) is coarser than \(\mathcal{T}_2\) iff the identity map \(\mathrm{id}_X: (X, \mathcal{T}_1) \to (X, \mathcal{T}_2)\) is open.


[点击展开翻译]

5.2.1.2 定理： 设 \(\mathcal{T}_1\) 和 \(\mathcal{T}_2\) 是集合 \(X\) 上的两个拓扑。则 \(\mathcal{T}_1\) 比 \(\mathcal{T}_2\) 粗，当且仅当恒等映射 \(\mathrm{id}_X: (X, \mathcal{T}_1) \to (X, \mathcal{T}_2)\) 是开映射。






5.2.2 Retractions and sections.






5.2.2.1 Definition: A topological section is a section in \(\mathsf{Top}\). A topological retraction is a retraction in \(\mathsf{Top}\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.2.2.1 定义： 拓扑截面是 \(\mathsf{Top}\) 中的截面。拓扑收缩是 \(\mathsf{Top}\) 中的收缩。










5.2.2.2 Definition: A subset \(A\) is called a retract of a topological space \(X\) if the inclusion map \(\iota: A \to X\) is a section.


[点击展开翻译]

5.2.2.2 定义： 如果包含映射 \(\iota: A \to X\) 是一个截面，则子集 \(A\) 称为拓扑空间 \(X\) 的收缩核 (retract)。










5.2.2.3 Remark: A retraction in the sense of [10, Definition 3.13] is a retraction in the sense of Definition 5.2.2.1 with the inclusion map as a section. If \(f: X \to Y\) is a retraction in the sense of Definition 5.2.2.1, with a continuous left inverse \(g: Y \to X\), we let \(\tilde{g}: Y \to g(Y)\) be the submap of \(g\) which is a homeomorphism and then \(\tilde{g} \circ f\) is retraction in the sense of [10, Definition 3.13] from \(X\) to \(g(Y)\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.2.2.3 备注： [10, 定义 3.13] 意义下的收缩是指定义 5.2.2.1 意义下且以包含映射为截面的收缩。如果 \(f: X \to Y\) 是定义 5.2.2.1 意义下的收缩，且具有连续左逆 \(g: Y \to X\)，我们设 \(\tilde{g}: Y \to g(Y)\) 为 \(g\) 的子映射且是一个同胚，则 \(\tilde{g} \circ f\) 是 [10, 定义 3.13] 意义下从 \(X\) 到 \(g(Y)\) 的收缩。










5.2.2.4 Remark: A continuous map \(f: X \to Y\) is a homeomorphism iff it is both a retraction and a section. In this case, its inverse \(g: Y \to X\) is also continuous, characterized by \(g \circ f = \mathrm{id}_X\) and \(f \circ g = \mathrm{id}_Y\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.2.2.4 备注： 一个连续映射 \(f: X \to Y\) 是同胚，当且仅当它既是收缩又是截面。在这种情况下，其逆映射 \(g: Y \to X\) 也是连续的，且满足 \(g \circ f = \mathrm{id}_X\) 以及 \(f \circ g = \mathrm{id}_Y\)。










5.2.2.5 Question: “Being a retract of” satisfies reflexivity and transitivity.


[点击展开翻译]

5.2.2.5 问题： “是…的收缩核”关系满足自反性和传递性。










5.2.2.6 Theorem: A topological space \(X\) is a retract of \(Y\) iff there is a continuous map \(i: X \to Y\) (or \(r: Y \to X\)) such that for any continuous map \(f: X \to Z\) there is a continuous map \(g: Y \to Z\) making the following diagram on the left (or on the right) commute, that is \(g \circ i = f\) (or \(f \circ r = g\)):





in which case the map \(g\) is unique.


[点击展开翻译]

5.2.2.6 定理： 一个拓扑空间 \(X\) 是 \(Y\) 的收缩核，当且仅当存在连续映射 \(i: X \to Y\)（或 \(r: Y \to X\)），使得对于任何连续映射 \(f: X \to Z\)，都存在唯一的连续映射 \(g: Y \to Z\) 使左侧（或右侧）的交换图表成立，即 \(g \circ i = f\)（或 \(f \circ r = g\)）。






[点击展开证明]

Proof: For the necessary condition, chase the diagram





For the sufficient condition, for the diagram on the left, choose \(f = \mathrm{id}_X\) and then \(g\) is a retraction; for the diagram on the right, this is an exercise. ■



5.2.3 Embeddings and quotient maps.
In \(\mathsf{Top}\), the extremal monomorphisms and epimorphisms have special names.






5.2.3.1 Definition: An embedding is an extremal monomorphism in \(\mathsf{Top}\). A quotient map is an extremal epimorphism in \(\mathsf{Top}\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.2.3.1 定义： 嵌入 (embedding) 是 \(\mathsf{Top}\) 中的极小单态射。商映射 (quotient map) 是 \(\mathsf{Top}\) 中的极大满态射。




Embeddings and quotient maps arise from topological subspaces and quotient spaces; see Section 8.2.2 and Section 8.4.2. We will see there that, not all sections are embeddings, and not all retractions are quotient maps. Therefore, in \(\mathsf{Top}\), monomorphisms, sections, and extremal monomorphisms are different, and epimorphisms, retractions, and extremal epimorphisms are also different.
crumbs: 5. MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES

title: MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES
section: 5.3 Closures and interiors | 闭包与内部
text:
5.3 Closures and interiors | 闭包与内部

5.3.1 Points with respect to a subset.






5.3.1.1 Definition: Let \(X\) be a topological space with \(p \in X\). An open neighborhood of \(p\) in \(X\) is an open subset \(U\) containing \(p\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.3.1.1 定义： 设 \(X\) 是一个拓扑空间且 \(p \in X\)。\(p\) 在 \(X\) 中的一个开邻域是一个包含 \(p\) 的开子集 \(U\)。










5.3.1.2 Proposition: Let \(p\) be a point of a topological space \(X\). A subset \(U\) is an open neighborhood of \(p\) in \(X\) iff \(U\) is open and a neighborhood of \(p\). A subset \(N\) is a neighborhood of \(p\) in \(X\) iff there is an open neighborhood \(U\) of \(p\) in \(X\) that is contained in \(N\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.3.1.2 命题： 设 \(p\) 是拓扑空间 \(X\) 中的一个点。子集 \(U\) 是 \(p\) 在 \(X\) 中的一个开邻域，当且仅当 \(U\) 是开集且是 \(p\) 的一个邻域。子集 \(N\) 是 \(p\) 在 \(X\) 中的一个邻域，当且仅当存在一个包含在 \(N\) 中的 \(p\) 的开邻域 \(U\)。










5.3.1.3 Definition: Let \(X\) be a topological space with \(p \in X\). A subset \(P\) is a deleted neighborhood of \(p\) in \(X\) if there is a neighborhood \(N\) of \(p\) in \(X\) with \(P = N \setminus \{p\}\). We denote by \(\mathcal{N}_p^\circ\) the collection of deleted neighborhoods of \(p\) in \(X\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.3.1.3 定义： 设 \(X\) 是一个拓扑空间且 \(p \in X\)。如果存在 \(p\) 在 \(X\) 中的一个邻域 \(N\) 使得 \(P = N \setminus \{p\}\)，则子集 \(P\) 称为 \(p\) 在 \(X\) 中的一个去心邻域。我们用 \(\mathcal{N}_p^\circ\) 表示 \(p\) 在 \(X\) 中所有去心邻域的族。




The characterization of closed subsets in Definition 2.1.2.7 can be generalized to any topological space. First we generalize Definition 2.1.2.6 and some related concepts, including the closure and the derived set that were considered by Cauchy. Then, a subset is closed iff it coincides with its closure.






5.3.1.4 Definition: Let \(X\) be a topological space with a subset \(A\). Let \(p \in X\) and of \(A, p\) is

an adherent point iff \(A \cap N \neq \emptyset\) for any \(N \in \mathcal{N}_{X,p}\);
a limit point or an accumulation point iff \(A \cap P \neq \emptyset\) for any \(P \in \mathcal{N}_p^\circ\);
an isolated point iff \(p \in A\) and \(A \cap P = \emptyset\) for some \(P \in \mathcal{N}_p^\circ\).

Then of \(A\),

the closure is the set of adherent points of \(A\), denoted by \(\mathrm{Cl}(A)\) or \(\bar{A}\) or \(A^-\);
the derived set is the set of accumulation points of \(A\), denoted by \(\mathrm{Der}(A)\) or \(A'\).



[点击展开翻译]

5.3.1.4 定义： 设 \(X\) 是一个拓扑空间，\(A\) 是其子集。对于 \(p \in X\)：

\(p\) 是 \(A\) 的触点 (adherent point)，当且仅当对于任何 \(N \in \mathcal{N}_{X,p}\)，都有 \(A \cap N \neq \emptyset\)；
\(p\) 是 \(A\) 的极限点或聚点 (accumulation point)，当且仅当对于任何 \(P \in \mathcal{N}_p^\circ\)，都有 \(A \cap P \neq \emptyset\)；
\(p\) 是 \(A\) 的孤立点，当且仅当 \(p \in A\) 且对于某个 \(P \in \mathcal{N}_p^\circ\)，有 \(A \cap P = \emptyset\)。

对于子集 \(A\)：

闭包是 \(A\) 的所有触点的集合，记作 \(\mathrm{Cl}(A)\) 或 \(\bar{A}\) 或 \(A^-\)；
导集是 \(A\) 的所有聚点的集合，记作 \(\mathrm{Der}(A)\) 或 \(A'\)。











5.3.1.5 Proposition: For any subset \(A\) of a topological space \(X\), we have

\(\mathrm{Cl}(A) = A \cup \mathrm{Der}(A)\);
the set of isolated points of \(A\) is \(A \setminus \mathrm{Der}(A)\).



[点击展开翻译]

5.3.1.5 命题： 对于拓扑空间 \(X\) 的任何子集 \(A\)，我们有：

\(\mathrm{Cl}(A) = A \cup \mathrm{Der}(A)\)；
\(A\) 的孤立点集为 \(A \setminus \mathrm{Der}(A)\)。





Let \(I\) be an interval in \(\mathbb{R}\) with endpoints \(a < b\). The points in \(\mathbb{R}\) can be classified into three types with respect to \(I\): the interior points of those in \(]a, b[\), the endpoints (or boundary points) \(a\) and \(b\), and the exterior points of those in \(]-\infty, a[ \cup ]b, \infty[\). Then purely interior points constitutes an open interval \(]a, b[\), while together with endpoints constitutes a closed interval \([a, b]\).


5.3.2 Closures and interiors.






5.3.2.1 Definition: Let \(X\) be a topological space with a subset \(A\) and \(p \in X\) and

\(p\) is an interior point of \(A\), iff \(A \supseteq N\) for some \(N \in \mathcal{N}_{X,p}\);
\(p\) is an exterior point of \(A\), iff \(A \cap N = \emptyset\) for some \(N \in \mathcal{N}_{X,p}\);
\(p\) is a boundary point of \(A\), iff \(A \cap N \neq \emptyset\) and \(A^c \cap N \neq \emptyset\) for any \(N \in \mathcal{N}_{X,p}\).

Then of \(A\),

the interior is the set of interior points of \(A\), denoted by \(\mathrm{Int}(A)\) or \(A^\circ\);
the exterior is the set of exterior points of \(A\), denoted by \(\mathrm{Ext}(A)\) or \(A^e\);
the boundary is the set of boundary points of \(A\), denoted by \(\mathrm{Bd}(A)\) or \(\partial A\) or \(A^b\).



[点击展开翻译]

5.3.2.1 定义： 设 \(X\) 是一个拓扑空间，\(A\) 是其子集，\(p \in X\)：

\(p\) 是 \(A\) 的内点 (interior point)，当且仅当对于某个 \(N \in \mathcal{N}_{X,p}\)，有 \(A \supseteq N\)；
\(p\) 是 \(A\) 的外点 (exterior point)，当且仅当对于某个 \(N \in \mathcal{N}_{X,p}\)，有 \(A \cap N = \emptyset\)；
\(p\) 是 \(A\) 的边界点 (boundary point)，当且仅当对于任何 \(N \in \mathcal{N}_{X,p}\)，都有 \(A \cap N \neq \emptyset\) 且 \(A^c \cap N \neq \emptyset\)。

对于 \(A\)：

内部是 \(A\) 的所有内点的集合，记作 \(\mathrm{Int}(A)\) 或 \(A^\circ\)；
外部是 \(A\) 的所有外点的集合，记作 \(\mathrm{Ext}(A)\) 或 \(A^e\)；
边界是 \(A\) 的所有边界点的集合，记作 \(\mathrm{Bd}(A)\) 或 \(\partial A\) 或 \(A^b\)。











5.3.2.2 Theorem: The closure of a subset \(A\) of a topological space \(X\) is the smallest closed subset of \(X\) containing \(A\); the interior of \(A\) is the largest open subset of \(X\) contained in \(A\); the exterior of a subset \(A\) is the largest open subset of \(X\) disjoint from \(A\). That is to say \[\mathrm{Cl}(A) = \bigcap_{A \subseteq C, C^c \in \mathcal{T}} C, \quad \mathrm{Int}(A) = \bigcup_{A \supseteq U \in \mathcal{T}} U, \quad \mathrm{Ext}(A) = \bigcup_{V \in \mathcal{T}, A \cap V = \emptyset} V \tag{41}\] where \(\mathcal{T}\) is the topology on \(X\) and \(\mathcal{C}\) is the collection of closed subsets of \(X\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.3.2.2 定理： 拓扑空间 \(X\) 的子集 \(A\) 的闭包是包含 \(A\) 的最小闭子集；\(A\) 的内部是包含在 \(A\) 中的最大开子集；子集 \(A\) 的外部是与 \(A\) 不相交的最大开子集。也就是说： \[\mathrm{Cl}(A) = \bigcap_{A \subseteq C, C^c \in \mathcal{T}} C, \quad \mathrm{Int}(A) = \bigcup_{A \supseteq U \in \mathcal{T}} U, \quad \mathrm{Ext}(A) = \bigcup_{V \in \mathcal{T}, A \cap V = \emptyset} V \tag{41}\] 其中 \(\mathcal{T}\) 是 \(X\) 上的拓扑，\(\mathcal{C}\) 是 \(X\) 的闭子集族。










5.3.2.3 Proposition: A subset \(A\) of a topological space \(X\) coincides with its closure iff it is closed; it coincides with its interior iff it is open. That is to say \[A \in \mathcal{T} \iff A = \mathrm{Int}(A) \iff \mathrm{Bd}(A) \subseteq A^c, \tag{42}\] \[A^c \in \mathcal{C} \iff A = \mathrm{Cl}(A) \iff \mathrm{Bd}(A) \subseteq A.\]


[点击展开翻译]

5.3.2.3 命题： 拓扑空间 \(X\) 的子集 \(A\) 与其闭包重合当且仅当它是闭集；与其内部重合当且仅当它是开集。即： \[A \in \mathcal{T} \iff A = \mathrm{Int}(A) \iff \mathrm{Bd}(A) \subseteq A^c, \tag{42}\] \[A^c \in \mathcal{C} \iff A = \mathrm{Cl}(A) \iff \mathrm{Bd}(A) \subseteq A.\]










5.3.2.4 Proposition: A set \(N\) is a neighborhood of \(x\) in \(X\) iff \(x\) is an interior point of \(N\), namely, \[N \in \mathcal{N}_x \iff x \in N^\circ. \tag{43}\]


[点击展开翻译]

5.3.2.4 命题： 集合 \(N\) 是 \(x\) 在 \(X\) 中的一个邻域，当且仅当 \(x\) 是 \(N\) 的一个内点，即： \[N \in \mathcal{N}_x \iff x \in N^\circ. \tag{43}\]










5.3.2.5 Proposition: For any subset \(A\) of a topological space \(X\), we have

\(\mathrm{Int}(A) \subseteq A \subseteq \mathrm{Cl}(A)\);
\(\mathrm{Cl}(A) = A \cup \mathrm{Bd}(A) = \mathrm{Int}(A) \sqcup \mathrm{Bd}(A)\);
\(X = \mathrm{Int}(A) \sqcup \mathrm{Bd}(A) \sqcup \mathrm{Ext}(A) = A^\circ \sqcup (\partial A \cap A) \sqcup (\partial A \setminus A) \sqcup A^e\);
\(\mathrm{Int}(A) = \mathrm{Cl}(A^c)^c, \mathrm{Ext}(A) = \mathrm{Cl}(A)^c, \mathrm{Bd}(A) = \mathrm{Cl}(A) \cap \mathrm{Cl}(A^c)\).



[点击展开翻译]

5.3.2.5 命题： 对于拓扑空间 \(X\) 的任何子集 \(A\)，我们有：

\(\mathrm{Int}(A) \subseteq A \subseteq \mathrm{Cl}(A)\)；
\(\mathrm{Cl}(A) = A \cup \mathrm{Bd}(A) = \mathrm{Int}(A) \sqcup \mathrm{Bd}(A)\)；
\(X = \mathrm{Int}(A) \sqcup \mathrm{Bd}(A) \sqcup \mathrm{Ext}(A) = A^\circ \sqcup (\partial A \cap A) \sqcup (\partial A \setminus A) \sqcup A^e\)；
\(\mathrm{Int}(A) = \mathrm{Cl}(A^c)^c, \mathrm{Ext}(A) = \mathrm{Cl}(A)^c, \mathrm{Bd}(A) = \mathrm{Cl}(A) \cap \mathrm{Cl}(A^c)\)。





From Proposition 5.3.2.5 one observe that the interior and exterior of a subset can be obtained by successively taking the closure and complement, and would be curious how many different subsets it can generate. The maximal number 14 of distinct subsets is due to Kuratowski; see Exercise 5.3.5.1.






5.3.2.6 Theorem: Let \(f: X \to Y\) be a map between topological spaces. The following are equivalent:

\(f\) is continuous;
\((\forall \text{ closed } B \subseteq Y)(f^{-1}(B) \text{ is closed in } X)\);
for some subbase \(\mathcal{S}_Y\) of the topology on \(Y\) we have \((\forall V \in \mathcal{S}_Y)(f^{-1}(V) \text{ is open in } X)\);
\(f\) is continuous at every point \(p \in X\);
for any subset \(A\) of \(X\) we have \(f(A^-) \subseteq (f(A))^-\);
for any subset \(B\) of \(Y\) we have \(f^{-1}(B)^- \subseteq f^{-1}(B^-)\);
for any subset \(B\) of \(Y\) we have \(f^{-1}(B^\circ) \subseteq f^{-1}(B)^\circ\).



[点击展开翻译]

5.3.2.6 定理： 设 \(f: X \to Y\) 是拓扑空间之间的映射。以下各项等价：

\(f\) 是连续的；
对 \(Y\) 的任意闭子集 \(B\)，\(f^{-1}(B)\) 在 \(X\) 中是闭集；
对于 \(Y\) 上拓扑的某个子基 \(\mathcal{S}_Y\)，对任意 \(V \in \mathcal{S}_Y\)，\(f^{-1}(V)\) 在 \(X\) 中是开集；
\(f\) 在 \(X\) 的每个点 \(p\) 处都连续；
对于 \(X\) 的任何子集 \(A\)，有 \(f(A^-) \subseteq (f(A))^-\)；
对于 \(Y\) 的任何子集 \(B\)，有 \(f^{-1}(B)^- \subseteq f^{-1}(B^-)\)；
对于 \(Y\) 的任何子集 \(B\)，有 \(f^{-1}(B^\circ) \subseteq f^{-1}(B)^\circ\)。







[点击展开证明]

Proof: We prove the equivalence of (2), (5), and (6) as an example, and uses the fact for any \(A \subseteq X\) and \(B \subseteq Y\), \[A \subseteq f^{-1}(f(A)), \quad B \supseteq f(f^{-1}(B)). \tag{44}\] If (2) is given, then for any \(A \subseteq X\), the preimage \(f^{-1}(f(A)^-)\) is closed in \(X\) and contains \(A\), then \(f^{-1}(f(A)^-) \supseteq A^-\). By taking the image, we obtain \[f(A^-) \subseteq f(f^{-1}(f(A)^-)) \subseteq f(A)^-, \tag{45}\] which implies (5). If (5) is given, then for any \(B \subseteq Y\), we have \[f(f^{-1}(B)^-) \subseteq f(f^{-1}(B))^- \subseteq B^-, \tag{46}\] which implies \(f^{-1}(B)^-) \subseteq f^{-1}(f(f^{-1}(B)^-)) \subseteq f^{-1}(B^-)\), which is (6). If (6) is given, then for any closed \(B \subseteq Y\), we have \[f^{-1}(B) \subseteq f^{-1}(B)^- \subseteq f^{-1}(B^-) = f^{-1}(B), \tag{47}\] which implies that \(f^{-1}(B)\) is closed in \(X\) and (2) holds. ■







5.3.2.7 Proposition: If \(f: X \to Y\) be a continuous map between topological spaces, then for any \(A \subseteq X\) and \(B \subseteq Y\) with \(B \supseteq f(A)\), the submap \(f|_{A,B}: A \to B\) is continuous.


[点击展开翻译]

5.3.2.7 命题： 若 \(f: X \to Y\) 是拓扑空间之间的连续映射，则对于满足 \(B \supseteq f(A)\) 的任何 \(A \subseteq X\) 和 \(B \subseteq Y\)，子映射 \(f|_{A,B}: A \to B\) 也是连续的。






5.3.3 Rational and irrational numbers.
The denseness of rational numbers is written in topology terms.






5.3.3.1 Definition: A subset \(A\) of a topological space \(X\) is dense if \(\mathrm{Cl}(A) = X\); it has empty interior if \(\mathrm{Int}(A) = \emptyset\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.3.3.1 定义： 拓扑空间 \(X\) 的子集 \(A\) 如果满足 \(\mathrm{Cl}(A) = X\)，则称其是稠密的 (dense)；如果满足 \(\mathrm{Int}(A) = \emptyset\)，则称其内部为空。










5.3.3.2 Proposition: The rationals \(\mathbb{Q}\) and irrationals \(\mathbb{R} \setminus \mathbb{Q}\) are dense in \(\mathbb{R}\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.3.3.2 命题： 有理数集 \(\mathbb{Q}\) 和无理数集 \(\mathbb{R} \setminus \mathbb{Q}\) 在 \(\mathbb{R}\) 中都是稠密的。






5.3.4 The Cantor set.






5.3.4.1 The Cantor set: Let \(C_0 = [0, 1]\). Let \(C_1\) be the set \(C_0 \setminus ]\frac{1}{3}, \frac{2}{3}[\) formed by deleting the middle third of \(C_0\). Form \(C_2\) by deleting the middle-thirds \(]\frac{1}{9}, \frac{2}{9}[\) and \(]\frac{7}{9}, \frac{8}{9}[\) from each component of \(C_1\). Inductively, we to define \[C_n = C_{n-1} - \bigcup_{k=0}^{3^{n-1}-1} \left] \frac{1 + 3k}{3^n}, \frac{2 + 3k}{3^n} \right[. \tag{48}\] The Cantor set is given by \(C = \bigcap_{n \in \mathbb{N}} C_n\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.3.4.1 Cantor 集： 设 \(C_0 = [0, 1]\)。令 \(C_1\) 为集合 \(C_0 \setminus ]\frac{1}{3}, \frac{2}{3}[\)，由删去 \(C_0\) 的中间三分之一构成。从 \(C_1\) 的每个连通分支中删去中间三分之一区间 \(]\frac{1}{9}, \frac{2}{9}[\) 和 \(]\frac{7}{9}, \frac{8}{9}[\) 得到 \(C_2\)。以此类推，我们归纳地定义： \[C_n = C_{n-1} - \bigcup_{k=0}^{3^{n-1}-1} \left] \frac{1 + 3k}{3^n}, \frac{2 + 3k}{3^n} \right[. \tag{48}\] Cantor 集由 \(C = \bigcap_{n \in \mathbb{N}} C_n\) 给出。




Note that each \(C_n\) Example 5.3.4.1 is the union of \(2^n\) disjoint closed intervals of length \(\frac{1}{3^n}\).






5.3.4.2 Proposition: The Cantor set \(C\) is closed in \(\mathbb{R}\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.3.4.2 命题： Cantor 集 \(C\) 在 \(\mathbb{R}\) 中是闭集。
crumbs: 5. MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES

title: MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES
text:
5.4 Hausdorff spaces | Hausdorff 空间






5.4.0.1 Definition: A topological space \(X\) is Hausdorff, iff for any distinct \(p, q \in X\) there are \(U \in \mathcal{N}_{X,p}\) and \(V \in \mathcal{N}_{X,q}\) such that \(U \cap V = \emptyset\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.4.0.1 定义： 一个拓扑空间 \(X\) 是 Hausdorff 空间，当且仅当对于任何不同的点 \(p, q \in X\)，都存在 \(U \in \mathcal{N}_{X,p}\) 和 \(V \in \mathcal{N}_{X,q}\)，使得 \(U \cap V = \emptyset\)。










5.4.0.2 Proposition: The Euclidean space \(\mathbb{R}^n\) is Hausdorff for \(n \in \mathbb{N}_0\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.4.0.2 命题： 对于 \(n \in \mathbb{N}_0\)，欧几里得空间 \(\mathbb{R}^n\) 是 Hausdorff 空间。
crumbs: 5. MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES

title: MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES
text:
5.5 Countable spaces | 可数空间






5.5.0.1 Definition: A topological space \(X\) is

separable iff it has a countable dense subset;
first countable iff it has a countable neighborhood base for every point;
second countable iff it has a countable base.



[点击展开翻译]

5.5.0.1 定义： 一个拓扑空间 \(X\) 称为：

可分的 (separable)，当且仅当它有一个可数的稠密子集；
第一可数的 (first countable)，当且仅当它的每个点都有一个可数邻域基；
第二可数的 (second countable)，当且仅当它有一个可数基。






可分的 (Separable)


严谨定义：空间 \(X\) 中存在一个子集 \(A\)，满足 \(A\) 是可数集，并且 \(A\) 的闭包是整个空间（即 \(\overline{A} = X\)）。
大白话理解：空间本身可能无限大、不可数（比如整条实数轴），但我能往里面撒一把“小米”（可数个点），这把小米能无限逼近空间中的任何一个位置。换句话说，空间里的任何一个非空开集，里面都至少包含一粒“小米”。
经典例子：实数集 \(\mathbb{R}\) 是可分的，因为有理数集 \(\mathbb{Q}\) 是可数的，且在 \(\mathbb{R}\) 中稠密（任何开区间里都含有理数）。


第一可数的 (First Countable)


严谨定义：空间 \(X\) 中的每一个点 \(x\)，都有一个可数的局部基（邻域基） \(\mathcal{B}_x = \{U_1, U_2, U_3, \dots\}\)。这意味着对于包含 \(x\) 的任何开集 \(V\)，总能在这个可数基里找到一个 \(U_n\)，使得 \(x \in U_n \subseteq V\)。
大白话理解：这是一个局部性质。站在空间里的任意一点，我只需要用按顺序排好的一列开集（比如越来越小的圈），就能完全描述向这个点“无限逼近”的过程。它最重要的作用是保证了序列 (sequences) 足够用来刻画极限和连续性（否则在更一般的空间里，必须用复杂的“网”去刻画）。
经典例子：任何度量空间都是第一可数的。对于任意点 \(x\)，取半径为 \(\frac{1}{n}\) (\(n \in \mathbb{N}\)) 的开球系列 \(\{B(x, \frac{1}{n})\}_{n=1}^\infty\)，这自然构成了一个可数邻域基。


第二可数的 (Second Countable)


严谨定义：整个拓扑空间 \(X\) 的拓扑 \(\mathcal{T}\) 有一个可数基 \(\mathcal{B}\)。
大白话理解：这是一个极强的全局性质。描述整个空间所有可能的形状，我只需要一本“页数可数的字典”（可数基）。空间里的任何一个开集，无论多么千奇百怪，都可以用这本字典里的词（基元素）并出来。
经典例子：\(\mathbb{R}^n\) 是第二可数的。它的可数基可以是：所有中心坐标为有理数、且半径也为有理数的开球的集合。

The base in Example 3.2.1.3 and neighborhood base in Example 3.2.2.5 can be distrilled.






5.5.0.2 Proposition: The Euclidean space \(\mathbb{R}^n\) is separable, first and second countable for \(n \in \mathbb{N}_0\).


[点击展开翻译]

5.5.0.2 命题： 对于 \(n \in \mathbb{N}_0\)，欧几里得空间 \(\mathbb{R}^n\) 是可分的，且满足第一和第二可数性公理。
crumbs: 5. MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES

title: MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES
text:
5.6 Topos. | Topos 范畴






5.6.0.1 Definition: An (elementary) topos is defined to be a category \(\mathsf{E}\) with the following properties:

\(\mathsf{E}\) has all finite limits;
\(\mathsf{E}\) has a subobject classifier;
\(\mathsf{E}\) is cartesian closed.



[点击展开翻译]

5.6.0.1 定义： 一个（初等）Topos 被定义为满足以下性质的范畴 \(\mathsf{E}\)：

\(\mathsf{E}\) 具有所有有限极限；
\(\mathsf{E}\) 具有一个子对象分类器；
\(\mathsf{E}\) 是笛卡尔封闭的。





We recall that requiring \(\mathsf{E}\) to be cartesian closed means requiring that each functor “product with \(b\)” (i.e., \(a \to a \times b\)) has for all \(b\) in \(\mathsf{E}\) a right adjoint \(c \to c^b\), so that \(\mathrm{hom}(a \times b, c) \cong \mathrm{hom}(a, c^b)\). In other words, \(\mathsf{E}\) has exponentials.






5.6.0.2 Example: The category \(\mathsf{Set}\) of all small sets is a topos and so is the category \(\mathsf{Set}^{\mathsf{C}^{\mathrm{op}}}\) of all set-valued contravariant functors on a small category \(\mathsf{C}\). Such a functor \(F : \mathsf{C}^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathsf{Set}\) is also called a presheaf.


[点击展开翻译]

5.6.0.2 示例： 所有小集合构成的范畴 \(\mathsf{Set}\) 是一个 Topos，小范畴 \(\mathsf{C}\) 上的所有集合值逆变函子构成的范畴 \(\mathsf{Set}^{\mathsf{C}^{\mathrm{op}}}\) 也是。此类函子 \(F : \mathsf{C}^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathsf{Set}\) 也被称为前层。




This is in reference to topology, where \(\mathsf{C}\) is the set of all open sets \(U\) of a topological space \(X\). In this case, a presheaf \(F\) assigns to each open set \(U\) a set \(F(U)\), with functorial properties for continuous maps \(U \to V\).






5.6.0.3 Example: Let \(F(U)\) be the set of all continuous real-valued functions on \(U\). In this case, \(F\) is said to be a sheaf (think of the sheaf of coefficients for a cohomology theory!).


[点击展开翻译]

5.6.0.3 示例： 设 \(F(U)\) 为 \(U\) 上所有连续实值函数的集合。在这种情况下，\(F\) 被称为一个层（可以联想上同调理论中的系数层！）。




This and other categories of sheaves play a central role in algebraic geometry and in algebraic topology; the word “topos” is evidently a derivative of the word “topology”, suggesting that a topological structure is essentially described by its topos of sheaves of \(\mathsf{Set}\).
This study of categories of sheaves on topological spaces and their generalization has led to the study of toposes (see Mac Lane–Moerdijk [1992]). In particular, various logical properties are reflected in the subobject classifiers \(Q\) of a topos. Under many circumstances, a topos provides an alternative view of the foundations of mathematics; for example, the use of “forcing” to prove the independence of the continuum hypothesis can be well organized in terms of constructions on toposes (see Mac Lane–Moerdijk [1992], Chapter VI). Also, suitable toposes can replace the category of sets as a foundation for mathematics.
The axioms for a topos have many useful consequences.






5.6.0.4 Proposition: Every topos has all finite colimits.


[点击展开翻译]

5.6.0.4 命题： 每个 Topos 都具有所有有限上极限。
crumbs: 5. MAPS BETWEEN AND SUBSETS OF TOPOLOGICAL SPACES

title: METRIZING TOPOLOGICAL SPACES
text:
18.1.0.1 Proposition: A metric space is compact iff it is sequentially compact iff it is limit point compact iff it is pseudocompact.


[点击展开翻译]

18.1.0.1 命题： 一个度量空间是紧的，当且仅当它是序列紧的，当且仅当它是极限点紧的，当且仅当它是伪紧的。










18.1.0.2 Proposition: A metric space is first countable.


[点击展开翻译]

18.1.0.2 命题： 度量空间是第一可数的。










18.1.0.3 Proposition: A metric space is Lindelöf iff it is separable iff it is second countable.


[点击展开翻译]

18.1.0.3 命题： 一个度量空间是 Lindelöf 的，当且仅当它是可分的，当且仅当它是第二可数的。










18.1.0.4 Proposition: A metric space is perfectly normal.


[点击展开翻译]

18.1.0.4 命题： 度量空间是完美正规的。

text:
18.1.0.1 Proposition: A metric space is compact iff it is sequentially compact iff it is limit point compact iff it is pseudocompact.


[点击展开翻译]

18.1.0.2 Proposition: A metric space is first countable.


[点击展开翻译]

18.1.0.3 Proposition: A metric space is Lindelöf iff it is separable iff it is second countable.


[点击展开翻译]

18.1.0.4 Proposition: A metric space is perfectly normal.

title: METRIZING TOPOLOGICAL SPACES
text:
18.2 Metrizability. | 可度量化性

18.2.1 Urysohn metrization theorem. | Urysohn 度量化定理






18.2.1.1 Definition: A topological space \(X\) is metrizable iff its topology is induced by a metric \(d\) on \(X\).


[点击展开翻译]

18.2.1.1 定义： 一个拓扑空间 \(X\) 是可度量化的 (metrizable)，当且仅当它的拓扑是由 \(X\) 上的一个度量 \(d\) 诱导的。










18.2.1.2 Definition: A topological space \(X\) is Tychonoff iff it is Frechét and every disjoint point and closed subset are separated by functions.


[点击展开翻译]

18.2.1.2 定义： 一个拓扑空间 \(X\) 是 Tychonoff 空间，当且仅当它是 Fréchet 的（即 \(T_1\) 的），并且每个不相交的点和闭子集都能被函数分离。










18.2.1.3 Lemma: The product of Tychonoff spaces is Tychonoff.


[点击展开翻译]

18.2.1.3 引理： Tychonoff 空间的乘积是 Tychonoff 的。






[点击展开证明]

Proof: Let \(X\) be a Tychonoff space and \(I\) an index set. To show that \(X^I\) is Tychonoff, note that if \(f_i\) are functions for \((x, U_i)\) and \(g = \sup_{i \in I} f_i\), then \(g\) is a function for \((x, \bigcap_{i \in I} U_i)\). If \(x \in X^I\) let \(U_i \in \mathcal{N}_{x_i}\) for \(i \in I\). If \(f\) is a function for \((x_i, U_i)\), then \(f \circ \text{pr}_i\) is a function for \((x, \text{pr}_i^{-1}(U_i))\). \(\blacksquare\)







18.2.1.4 Lemma: A topological space \(X\) is Tychonoff iff it is homeomorphic to a subspace of \([0, 1]^{C(X; [0,1])}\).


[点击展开翻译]

18.2.1.4 引理： 拓扑空间 \(X\) 是 Tychonoff 空间，当且仅当它同胚于 \([0, 1]^{C(X; [0,1])}\) 的一个子空间。










18.2.1.5 Lemma: Let \(\mathcal{F} \subseteq C(X; [0, 1])\). Then the evaluation map \(\text{ev}: X \to [0, 1]^\mathcal{F}\), \(p \mapsto \text{ev}_p\)

is continuous;
is injective iff \(\mathcal{F}\) separates points of \(X\); and
is open onto its image if \(\mathcal{F}\) separates points from disjoint closed subsets of \(X\).



[点击展开翻译]

18.2.1.5 引理： 设 \(\mathcal{F} \subseteq C(X; [0, 1])\)。那么求值映射 \(\text{ev}: X \to [0, 1]^\mathcal{F}\)，\(p \mapsto \text{ev}_p\)

是连续的；
是单射，当且仅当 \(\mathcal{F}\) 分离 \(X\) 的点；并且
到其像上是开映射，当且仅当 \(\mathcal{F}\) 将点与 \(X\) 中不相交的闭子集分离。







[点击展开证明]

Proof: Since for any \(f \in \mathcal{F}\), \(\text{pr}_f \circ \text{ev} = f\) is continuous, the map \(\text{ev}\) is continuous. The map \(\text{ev}\) is injective iff for any distinct \(p, q \in X\), there is \(f \in \mathcal{F}\) with \(f(p) = \text{ev}_p(f) \neq \text{ev}_q(f) = f(q)\), which exactly means that \(\mathcal{F}\) separates points of \(X\). If the map \(\text{ev}\) separates points from disjoint closed subsets of \(X\), then for any \(p \in X\) and any \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\), there is \(f \in \mathcal{F}\) with \(f(p) = 0\) and \(f|_{X \setminus U} = 1\). In particular, \(V = \text{pr}_f^{-1}([0, 1[) = \text{ev}(f^{-1}([0, 1[))\) is an open neighborhood of \(\text{ev}_p\) in \(\text{ev}(X)\), which implies that \(\text{ev}\) is open onto its image. \(\blacksquare\)







18.2.1.6 Urysohn metrization theorem: Any regular second countable topological space is metrizable.


[点击展开翻译]

18.2.1.6 Urysohn 度量化定理： 任何正则的第二可数拓扑空间都是可度量化的。






[点击展开证明]

Proof: Let \(X\) be a regular second countable topological space with \(\mathcal{B}\) a countable topological base. Note that second countablility implies Lindelöfness, which together with regularity implies normality, and we have Urysohn lemma. For any \(U, V \in \mathcal{B}\) with \(\overline{U} \subseteq V\) choose \(f_{U,V} \in C(X; [0, 1])\) with \(f_{U,V}|_{\overline{U}} = 0\) and \(f_{U,V}|_{X \setminus V} = 1\), and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be the countable collection of such \(f_{U,V}\). If \(B \in \mathcal{P}(X)\) is closed and \(p \in X \setminus B\), choose \(V \in \mathcal{B}\) with \(p \in V \subseteq X \setminus B\) and by regularity choose \(U \in \mathcal{B}\) with \(p \in \overline{U} \subseteq V\). Then \(f_{U,V}\) separates \(p\) and \(B\). By the Frechètness single points are closed in \(X\). We conclude that \(\mathcal{F}\) separates points and separates points from disjoint closed subsets of \(X\). By Lemma 18.2.1.5, the evaluation map \(\text{ev}: X \to [0, 1]^\mathcal{F}\) is an embedding into a metrizable space and then \(X\) is metrizable. \(\blacksquare\)



18.2.2 Smirnov metrization theorem. | Smirnov 度量化定理






18.2.2.1 Definition: A topological space \(X\) is locally metrizable iff it has an open cover consisting of metrizable subspaces.


[点击展开翻译]

18.2.2.1 定义： 拓扑空间 \(X\) 是局部可度量化的 (locally metrizable)，当且仅当它有一个由可度量化子空间组成的开覆盖。










18.2.2.2 Nagata–Smirnov metrization theorem: Any topological space is metrizable iff it is a regular with a countably locally finite base.


[点击展开翻译]

18.2.2.2 Nagata–Smirnov 度量化定理： 任何拓扑空间是可度量化的，当且仅当它是一个具有可数局部有限基的正则空间。










18.2.2.3 Smirnov metrization theorem: Any topological space is metrizable iff it is a paracompact Hausdorff locally metrizable space.


[点击展开翻译]

18.2.2.3 Smirnov 度量化定理： 任何拓扑空间是可度量化的，当且仅当它是一个仿紧的 Hausdorff 局部可度量化空间。






[点击展开证明]

Proof: If a topological space \(X\) is metrizable, it is locally metrizable, paracompact, and Hausdorff. Suppose \(X\) is a paracompact Hausdorff locally metrizable space. Let \(\mathcal{A}\) be an open cover of \(X\) consisting of metrizable subspaces. By paracompactness of \(X\), the open cover \(\mathcal{A}\) has a locally finite open refinement \(\mathcal{A}_0\). Since every \(U \in \mathcal{A}_0\) is metrizable we let \(d_U\) be the metric on \(U\) generating the subspace topology. For any \(p \in U\) and \(r > 0\), let \(B_U(p, r) = B_{d_U}(p, r)\), which is open in \(U\) and hence open in \(X\). For any \(m \in \mathbb{N}\), let \(\mathcal{A}_m\) be the cover of \(X\) by open balls of radius \(\frac{1}{m}\): \[\mathcal{A}_m = \left\{ B_U\left(p, \frac{1}{m}\right) : p \in U, U \in \mathcal{A}_0 \right\}. \qquad (257)\] Let \(\mathcal{D}_m\) be a locally finite open refinement of \(\mathcal{A}_m\) that covers \(X\) and let \(\mathcal{D} = \bigcup_{m=1}^\infty \mathcal{D}_m\). Then \(\mathcal{D}\) is countably locally finite. We claim that \(\mathcal{D}\) is a base for the topology on \(X\), and then by Theorem 18.2.2.2 \(X\) is metrizable. Let \(p \in X\) and \(O \in \mathcal{N}_p\). Let \(\{U_1, ..., U_N\}\) be the finitely many elements of \(\mathcal{A}_0\) that have \(p\). Since \(O \cap U_i\) is a neighborhood of \(p\) in \(U_i\), there is \(\varepsilon_i > 0\) such that \(B_{U_i}(p, \varepsilon_i) \subseteq O \cap U_i\). Choose \(m \in \mathbb{N}\) such that \(\frac{2}{m} < \min\{\varepsilon_1, ..., \varepsilon_N\}\). Since \(\mathcal{D}_m\) covers \(X\), there is \(V \in \mathcal{D}_m\) having \(p\). Since \(\mathcal{D}_m\) refines \(\mathcal{A}_m\), there is an element \(B_W(q, \frac{1}{m}) \in \mathcal{A}_m\) with \(W \in \mathcal{A}_0\) and \(q \in W\) and \(V \subseteq B_W(q, \frac{1}{m})\). Then \(p \in W\) and \(W = U_i\) for some \(1 \le i \le N\). Then \(V \subseteq B_{U_i}(q, \frac{1}{m}) \subseteq B_{U_i}(p, \varepsilon_i) \subseteq O\). Thus, \(\mathcal{D}\) is a base for the topology on \(X\). \(\blacksquare\)

title: LENGTH SPACES
text:
Intuitively, a length structure consists of a class of admissible paths for which we can measure their length, and the length itself, which is a correspondence assigning a nonnegative number to every path from the class. Both the class and the correspondence have to possess several natural properties.
Recall that a path \(\gamma\) in a topological space \(X\) is a continuous map \(\gamma: I \to X\) defined on an interval \(I \subseteq \mathbb{R}\).






16.1.1.1 Definition: Length structures: A length structure on a topological space \(X\) is a class \(\mathcal{A}\) of admissible paths, which is a subset of all continuous paths in \(X\), together with a length functional \(L: \mathcal{A} \to [0, \infty]\). The class \(\mathcal{A}\) is closed under these operations:

Restrictions. If \(\gamma: I \to X\) is an admissible path and \([a, b] \subseteq I\), then the restriction \(\gamma|_{[a,b]}\) is also admissible.
Concatenations. If a path \(\gamma: [a, b] \to X\) is such that its restrictions \(\gamma|_{[a,c]}\) and \(\gamma|_{[c,b]}\) are both admissible paths, then so is \(\gamma\).
Linear reparameterizations. For an admissible path \(\gamma: I \to X\) and a homeomorphism \(\varphi: J \to I\) of the form \(\varphi(t) = at + b\) for some \(a, b \in \mathbb{R}\), the composition \(\gamma \circ \varphi\) is also an admissible path. The functional \(L\) possesses the following properties, denoting \(L(\gamma, a, b) = -L(\gamma, b, a) = L(\gamma|_{[a,b]})\) for any \([a, b] \subseteq I\):
Additivity. \(L(\gamma, a, b) = L(\gamma, a, c) + L(\gamma, c, b)\) for any \(a, b, c \in I\).
Continuous dependence on parameter. For a path \(\gamma: I \to X\), the function \(L(\gamma, \cdot, \cdot): I^2 \to [0, \infty]\) is continuous.
Invariance under reparameterization. \(L(\gamma \circ \varphi) = L(\gamma)\) for a linear homeomorphism \(\varphi\). The length structures agree with the topology of \(X\) in the following sense: for a neighborhood \(U_p\) of a point \(p\), the length of paths connecting \(p\) with points of the complement of \(U_p\) is separated from zero: \(\inf\{L(\gamma) : \gamma(a) = p, \gamma(b) \in X \setminus U_p\} > 0\).



[点击展开翻译]

16.1.1.1 定义： 长度结构 (Length structures)：拓扑空间 \(X\) 上的长度结构是容许路径的一个类 \(\mathcal{A}\)（它是 \(X\) 中所有连续路径的子集），连同一个长度泛函 (length functional) \(L: \mathcal{A} \to [0, \infty]\)。类 \(\mathcal{A}\) 在以下运算下是封闭的：

限制 (Restrictions)。如果 \(\gamma: I \to X\) 是一条容许路径且 \([a, b] \subseteq I\)，那么限制 \(\gamma|_{[a,b]}\) 也是容许的。
拼接 (Concatenations)。如果路径 \(\gamma: [a, b] \to X\) 使得它的限制 \(\gamma|_{[a,c]}\) 和 \(\gamma|_{[c,b]}\) 都是容许路径，那么 \(\gamma\) 也是容许的。
线性重新参数化 (Linear reparameterizations)。对于容许路径 \(\gamma: I \to X\) 和形如 \(\varphi(t) = at + b\)（其中 \(a, b \in \mathbb{R}\)）的同胚 \(\varphi: J \to I\)，复合 \(\gamma \circ \varphi\) 也是一条容许路径。 泛函 \(L\) 具有以下性质，对于任何 \([a, b] \subseteq I\)，记 \(L(\gamma, a, b) = -L(\gamma, b, a) = L(\gamma|_{[a,b]})\)：
可加性 (Additivity)。对于任意 \(a, b, c \in I\)，\(L(\gamma, a, b) = L(\gamma, a, c) + L(\gamma, c, b)\)。
对参数的连续依赖性 (Continuous dependence on parameter)。对于路径 \(\gamma: I \to X\)，函数 \(L(\gamma, \cdot, \cdot): I^2 \to [0, \infty]\) 是连续的。
重新参数化下的不变性 (Invariance under reparameterization)。对于线性同胚 \(\varphi\)，\(L(\gamma \circ \varphi) = L(\gamma)\)。 长度结构在以下意义上与 \(X\) 的拓扑相容：对于点 \(p\) 的邻域 \(U_p\)，连接 \(p\) 与 \(U_p\) 的补集中点的路径的长度有正的下界：\(\inf\{L(\gamma) : \gamma(a) = p, \gamma(b) \in X \setminus U_p\} > 0\)。





In fact, all reasonable length structures are invariant under arbitrary reparameterizations: \(L(\gamma \circ \varphi) = L(\gamma)\) for any homeomorphism \(\varphi\) such that both \(\gamma\) and \(\gamma \circ \varphi\) are admissible. However, it is not necessary to verify this in the beginning.






16.1.1.2 Remark: Every natural class of paths comes with its own class of reparameterizations. For example, consider the class of all continuous paths and the class of homeomorphisms, the class of piecewise smooth paths and the class of diffeomorphisms. We only require that this class of reparameterizations includes all linear maps.


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.1.2 备注： 每个自然的路径类都伴随着它自己的重新参数化类。例如，考虑所有连续路径的类和同胚的类，分段光滑路径的类和微分同胚的类。我们只要求这个重新参数化类包含所有线性映射。




16.1.1.3 Example: Examples of classes of admissible paths include: all continuous paths; piecewise smooth paths (on a smooth manifold); broken lines in \(\mathbb{R}^n\).



Once we have a length structure, we are ready to define a metric associated with the structure.






16.1.2.1 Definition: Let \(X\) be a Hausdorff space with a length structure \((\mathcal{A}, L)\). For two points \(p, q \in X\) the associated distance between them is the infimum of lengths of admissible paths connecting these points: \[d_L(p, q) = \inf\{L(\gamma) : \gamma: [a, b] \to X, \gamma \in \mathcal{A}, \gamma(a) = p, \gamma(b) = q\}. \qquad (246)\]


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.1 定义： 设 \(X\) 是一个带有长度结构 \((\mathcal{A}, L)\) 的 Hausdorff 空间。对于两个点 \(p, q \in X\)，它们之间的关联距离是连接这两点的容许路径长度的下确界： \[d_L(p, q) = \inf\{L(\gamma) : \gamma: [a, b] \to X, \gamma \in \mathcal{A}, \gamma(a) = p, \gamma(b) = q\}. \qquad (246)\]










16.1.2.2 Remark: If it is clear from the context which length structure \(L\) gives rise to \(d_L\), we usually drop \(L\) in the notation \(d_L\).


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.2 备注： 如果从上下文中可以清楚地看出是哪个长度结构 \(L\) 导出了 \(d_L\)，我们通常在记号 \(d_L\) 中省略 \(L\)。










16.1.2.3 Proposition: Let \(X\) be a Hausdorff space with a length structure \((\mathcal{A}, L)\). The associated distance \(d_L\) is a (generalized) metric on \(X\).


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.3 命题： 设 \(X\) 是带有长度结构 \((\mathcal{A}, L)\) 的 Hausdorff 空间。关联距离 \(d_L\) 是 \(X\) 上的一个（广义）度量。










16.1.2.4 Remark: Note that \(d_L\) is not necessarily a finite metric. For instance, if \(X\) is a disconnected union of two components, no continuous path can go from one component to the other and therefore the distance between points of different components is infinite. On the other hand, there may be points such that continuous paths connecting them exist but all have infinite length.


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.4 备注： 注意 \(d_L\) 不一定是有限度量。例如，如果 \(X\) 是两个连通分支的不连通并集，没有连续路径可以从一个分支走到另一个分支，因此不同分支的点之间的距离是无穷大。另一方面，也可能存在这样的点：连接它们的连续路径存在，但长度都是无穷大。










16.1.2.5 Definition: Let \(X\) be a Hausdorff space with a length structure \((\mathcal{A}, L)\). Two points \(p, q \in X\) belong to the same accessibility component if they can be connected by a path of finite length.


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.5 定义： 设 \(X\) 是带有长度结构 \((\mathcal{A}, L)\) 的 Hausdorff 空间。如果两点 \(p, q \in X\) 可以被一条有限长度的路径连接，则它们属于同一个可达分支 (accessibility component)。










16.1.2.6 Lemma: Let \(X\) be a Hausdorff space with a length structure \((\mathcal{A}, L)\). Admissible paths of finite length are continuous in the metric topology induced by \(d_L\).


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.6 引理： 设 \(X\) 是带有长度结构 \((\mathcal{A}, L)\) 的 Hausdorff 空间。有限长度的容许路径在由 \(d_L\) 诱导的度量拓扑中是连续的。

title: LENGTH SPACES
text:
Intuitively, a length structure consists of a class of admissible paths for which we can measure their length, and the length itself, which is a correspondence assigning a nonnegative number to every path from the class. Both the class and the correspondence have to possess several natural properties.
Recall that a path \(\gamma\) in a topological space \(X\) is a continuous map \(\gamma: I \to X\) defined on an interval \(I \subseteq \mathbb{R}\).






16.1.1.1 Definition: Length structures: A length structure on a topological space \(X\) is a class \(\mathcal{A}\) of admissible paths, which is a subset of all continuous paths in \(X\), together with a length functional \(L: \mathcal{A} \to [0, \infty]\). The class \(\mathcal{A}\) is closed under these operations:

Restrictions. If \(\gamma: I \to X\) is an admissible path and \([a, b] \subseteq I\), then the restriction \(\gamma|_{[a,b]}\) is also admissible.
Concatenations. If a path \(\gamma: [a, b] \to X\) is such that its restrictions \(\gamma|_{[a,c]}\) and \(\gamma|_{[c,b]}\) are both admissible paths, then so is \(\gamma\).
Linear reparameterizations. For an admissible path \(\gamma: I \to X\) and a homeomorphism \(\varphi: J \to I\) of the form \(\varphi(t) = at + b\) for some \(a, b \in \mathbb{R}\), the composition \(\gamma \circ \varphi\) is also an admissible path. The functional \(L\) possesses the following properties, denoting \(L(\gamma, a, b) = -L(\gamma, b, a) = L(\gamma|_{[a,b]})\) for any \([a, b] \subseteq I\):
Additivity. \(L(\gamma, a, b) = L(\gamma, a, c) + L(\gamma, c, b)\) for any \(a, b, c \in I\).
Continuous dependence on parameter. For a path \(\gamma: I \to X\), the function \(L(\gamma, \cdot, \cdot): I^2 \to [0, \infty]\) is continuous.
Invariance under reparameterization. \(L(\gamma \circ \varphi) = L(\gamma)\) for a linear homeomorphism \(\varphi\). The length structures agree with the topology of \(X\) in the following sense: for a neighborhood \(U_p\) of a point \(p\), the length of paths connecting \(p\) with points of the complement of \(U_p\) is separated from zero: \(\inf\{L(\gamma) : \gamma(a) = p, \gamma(b) \in X \setminus U_p\} > 0\).



[点击展开翻译]

16.1.1.1 定义： 长度结构 (Length structures)：拓扑空间 \(X\) 上的长度结构是容许路径的一个类 \(\mathcal{A}\)（它是 \(X\) 中所有连续路径的子集），连同一个长度泛函 (length functional) \(L: \mathcal{A} \to [0, \infty]\)。类 \(\mathcal{A}\) 在以下运算下是封闭的：

限制 (Restrictions)。如果 \(\gamma: I \to X\) 是一条容许路径且 \([a, b] \subseteq I\)，那么限制 \(\gamma|_{[a,b]}\) 也是容许的。
拼接 (Concatenations)。如果路径 \(\gamma: [a, b] \to X\) 使得它的限制 \(\gamma|_{[a,c]}\) 和 \(\gamma|_{[c,b]}\) 都是容许路径，那么 \(\gamma\) 也是容许的。
线性重新参数化 (Linear reparameterizations)。对于容许路径 \(\gamma: I \to X\) 和形如 \(\varphi(t) = at + b\)（其中 \(a, b \in \mathbb{R}\)）的同胚 \(\varphi: J \to I\)，复合 \(\gamma \circ \varphi\) 也是一条容许路径。 泛函 \(L\) 具有以下性质，对于任何 \([a, b] \subseteq I\)，记 \(L(\gamma, a, b) = -L(\gamma, b, a) = L(\gamma|_{[a,b]})\)：
可加性 (Additivity)。对于任意 \(a, b, c \in I\)，\(L(\gamma, a, b) = L(\gamma, a, c) + L(\gamma, c, b)\)。
对参数的连续依赖性 (Continuous dependence on parameter)。对于路径 \(\gamma: I \to X\)，函数 \(L(\gamma, \cdot, \cdot): I^2 \to [0, \infty]\) 是连续的。
重新参数化下的不变性 (Invariance under reparameterization)。对于线性同胚 \(\varphi\)，\(L(\gamma \circ \varphi) = L(\gamma)\)。 长度结构在以下意义上与 \(X\) 的拓扑相容：对于点 \(p\) 的邻域 \(U_p\)，连接 \(p\) 与 \(U_p\) 的补集中点的路径的长度有正的下界：\(\inf\{L(\gamma) : \gamma(a) = p, \gamma(b) \in X \setminus U_p\} > 0\)。





In fact, all reasonable length structures are invariant under arbitrary reparameterizations: \(L(\gamma \circ \varphi) = L(\gamma)\) for any homeomorphism \(\varphi\) such that both \(\gamma\) and \(\gamma \circ \varphi\) are admissible. However, it is not necessary to verify this in the beginning.






16.1.1.2 Remark: Every natural class of paths comes with its own class of reparameterizations. For example, consider the class of all continuous paths and the class of homeomorphisms, the class of piecewise smooth paths and the class of diffeomorphisms. We only require that this class of reparameterizations includes all linear maps.


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.1.2 备注： 每个自然的路径类都伴随着它自己的重新参数化类。例如，考虑所有连续路径的类和同胚的类，分段光滑路径的类和微分同胚的类。我们只要求这个重新参数化类包含所有线性映射。




16.1.1.3 Example: Examples of classes of admissible paths include: all continuous paths; piecewise smooth paths (on a smooth manifold); broken lines in \(\mathbb{R}^n\).



Once we have a length structure, we are ready to define a metric associated with the structure.






16.1.2.1 Definition: Let \(X\) be a Hausdorff space with a length structure \((\mathcal{A}, L)\). For two points \(p, q \in X\) the associated distance between them is the infimum of lengths of admissible paths connecting these points: \[d_L(p, q) = \inf\{L(\gamma) : \gamma: [a, b] \to X, \gamma \in \mathcal{A}, \gamma(a) = p, \gamma(b) = q\}. \qquad (246)\]


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.1 定义： 设 \(X\) 是一个带有长度结构 \((\mathcal{A}, L)\) 的 Hausdorff 空间。对于两个点 \(p, q \in X\)，它们之间的关联距离是连接这两点的容许路径长度的下确界： \[d_L(p, q) = \inf\{L(\gamma) : \gamma: [a, b] \to X, \gamma \in \mathcal{A}, \gamma(a) = p, \gamma(b) = q\}. \qquad (246)\]










16.1.2.2 Remark: If it is clear from the context which length structure \(L\) gives rise to \(d_L\), we usually drop \(L\) in the notation \(d_L\).


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.2 备注： 如果从上下文中可以清楚地看出是哪个长度结构 \(L\) 导出了 \(d_L\)，我们通常在记号 \(d_L\) 中省略 \(L\)。










16.1.2.3 Proposition: Let \(X\) be a Hausdorff space with a length structure \((\mathcal{A}, L)\). The associated distance \(d_L\) is a (generalized) metric on \(X\).


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.3 命题： 设 \(X\) 是带有长度结构 \((\mathcal{A}, L)\) 的 Hausdorff 空间。关联距离 \(d_L\) 是 \(X\) 上的一个（广义）度量。










16.1.2.4 Remark: Note that \(d_L\) is not necessarily a finite metric. For instance, if \(X\) is a disconnected union of two components, no continuous path can go from one component to the other and therefore the distance between points of different components is infinite. On the other hand, there may be points such that continuous paths connecting them exist but all have infinite length.


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.4 备注： 注意 \(d_L\) 不一定是有限度量。例如，如果 \(X\) 是两个连通分支的不连通并集，没有连续路径可以从一个分支走到另一个分支，因此不同分支的点之间的距离是无穷大。另一方面，也可能存在这样的点：连接它们的连续路径存在，但长度都是无穷大。










16.1.2.5 Definition: Let \(X\) be a Hausdorff space with a length structure \((\mathcal{A}, L)\). Two points \(p, q \in X\) belong to the same accessibility component if they can be connected by a path of finite length.


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.5 定义： 设 \(X\) 是带有长度结构 \((\mathcal{A}, L)\) 的 Hausdorff 空间。如果两点 \(p, q \in X\) 可以被一条有限长度的路径连接，则它们属于同一个可达分支 (accessibility component)。










16.1.2.6 Lemma: Let \(X\) be a Hausdorff space with a length structure \((\mathcal{A}, L)\). Admissible paths of finite length are continuous in the metric topology induced by \(d_L\).


[点击展开翻译]

16.1.2.6 引理： 设 \(X\) 是带有长度结构 \((\mathcal{A}, L)\) 的 Hausdorff 空间。有限长度的容许路径在由 \(d_L\) 诱导的度量拓扑中是连续的。

title: LENGTH SPACES
section: 16.2 Topology associated to the length structure. | 关联于长度结构的拓扑
text:
16.2 Topology associated to the length structure. | 关联于长度结构的拓扑
This lemma deals with the topology determined by the metric \(d_L\) rather than the original topology on \(X\).






16.2.0.1 Proposition: The topology determined by \(d_L\) is finer than that of \(X\).


[点击展开翻译]

16.2.0.1 命题： 由 \(d_L\) 决定的拓扑比 \(X\) 的拓扑更细。










16.2.0.2 Definition: Length spaces: A metric obtained as the distance function associated to a length structure is called an intrinsic, or length, metric. A metric space whose metric is intrinsic is called a length space.


[点击展开翻译]

16.2.0.2 定义： 长度空间 (Length spaces)：作为关联于长度结构的距离函数而获得的度量称为内蕴度量 (intrinsic metric) 或长度度量。其度量是内蕴度量的度量空间称为长度空间 (length space)。










16.2.0.3 Remark: Not every metric can arise as a length metric. Even if \((X, d)\) is a length space and \(A \subseteq X\), the restriction of \(d|_A\) is not necessarily intrinsic. For example, consider a circle in the plane. Moreover, not every metrizable topology can be induced by intrinsic metrics.


[点击展开翻译]

16.2.0.3 备注： 并非所有的度量都能作为长度度量出现。即使 \((X, d)\) 是一个长度空间且 \(A \subseteq X\)，限制 \(d|_A\) 也不一定是内蕴的。例如，考虑平面上的一个圆。此外，并非所有可度量化的拓扑都可以由内蕴度量诱导。




16.2.0.4 Example: (1) The rationals \(\mathbb{Q}\) is not homeomorphic to a length space. (2) The space \(X = \{(x, y) : y = \sin \frac{1}{x}, x > 0\} \cup \mathbb{R} \times \{0\} \subseteq \mathbb{R}^2\) is not homeomorphic to a length space.
There can be more delicate reasons why a topological space may be not homeomorphic to a length space.
[16, Exercises 2.1.8] 16.2.0.5 Example: Consider the union of segments \[X = \bigcup_{n \in \mathbb{N}} \left[ (0,0), \left(\cos \frac{1}{n}, \sin \frac{1}{n}\right) \right] \cup [(0,0), (1,0)] \subseteq \mathbb{R}^2. \qquad (247)\] This topological space is not homeomorphic to a length space.






16.2.0.6 Proposition: A length space is locally-path connected.


[点击展开翻译]

16.2.0.6 命题： 长度空间是局部路径连通的。










16.2.0.7 Remark: The infimum in Definition 16.1.2.1 cannot be replaced by the minimum since there may be no shortest path between two points.


[点击展开翻译]

16.2.0.7 备注： 定义 16.1.2.1 中的下确界不能被最小值代替，因为两点之间可能不存在最短路径。




For instance, consider the Euclidean plane with an open segment removed; then for the endpoints of the segment, the shortest path does not exist: it is just removed. Still, its length can be approximated with a given precision by other paths connecting the points. Such situations rarely arise in “real-life” examples; in most cases they will be also prohibited by imposing completeness-compactness type assumptions. On the other hand, existence of shortest paths helps to avoid tedious and nonessential complications. For the simplicity of our exposition we will often restrict ourselves to complete length structures.

title: LENGTH SPACES
text:
16.3 Geodesics. | 测地线

16.3.1 Geodesics. | 测地线






16.3.1.1 Definition: Geodesics: A curve \(\gamma: [a, b] \to X\) is a called a shortest path if its length is minimal among the curves with the same endpoints; that is \(L(\eta) \ge L(\gamma)\) for any curve \(\eta\) connecting \(\gamma(a)\) and \(\gamma(b)\).


[点击展开翻译]

16.3.1.1 定义： 测地线： 如果曲线 \(\gamma: [a, b] \to X\) 的长度在具有相同端点的曲线中是最小的，那么它被称为最短路径 (shortest path)；也就是说，对于任何连接 \(\gamma(a)\) 和 \(\gamma(b)\) 的曲线 \(\eta\)，都有 \(L(\eta) \ge L(\gamma)\)。










16.3.1.2 Definition: Geodesics: Let \(X\) be a length space. A curve \(\gamma: I \to X\) is called a geodesic if for every \(t \in I\) there exists a neighborhood \(J\) of \(t\) in \(I\) such that \(\gamma|_J\) is a shortest path.


[点击展开翻译]

16.3.1.2 定义： 测地线： 设 \(X\) 是一个长度空间。曲线 \(\gamma: I \to X\) 被称为测地线 (geodesic)，如果对于每一个 \(t \in I\)，在 \(I\) 中都存在一个 \(t\) 的邻域 \(J\)，使得 \(\gamma|_J\) 是一条最短路径。










16.3.1.3 Remark: The example of the sphere shows that there are geodesics that are not shortest paths: whereas every segment of a great circle on a sphere is a geodesic, it fails to be shortest as soon as it is longer than half of the equator. Although the spherical example suggests that a shortest path between two points should be unique at least locally, this is also not true in general. To see an example, consider the surface of a cube with its intrinsic metric induced by the Euclidean metric of its ambient space. We suggest that the reader shows that any neighborhood of a vertex contains points with multiple shortest paths between them. Another natural conjecture that turns out to be wrong is that a limit of geodesics is a geodesic; give a counterexample to this on the surface of the cube. We will see later that these phenomena are caused by nonsmoothness of the surface and that indeed such things never happen to smooth examples.


[点击展开翻译]

16.3.1.3 备注： 球面的例子表明，存在不是最短路径的测地线：虽然球面上大圆的每一段都是测地线，但一旦它长于赤道的一半，就不再是最短的。尽管球面的例子暗示两点之间的最短路径至少在局部应该是唯一的，但这在一般情况下也是不成立的。为了看一个例子，考虑一个立方体的表面，其内蕴度量由其所在空间的欧几里得度量诱导。我们建议读者证明，顶点的任何邻域都包含它们之间具有多条最短路径的点。另一个被证明是错误的自然猜想是，测地线的极限是测地线；请在立方体表面上给出一个反例。我们稍后将看到，这些现象是由表面的不光滑性引起的，实际上在光滑的例子中绝对不会发生这种事情。




It is clear that a shortest path always admits a natural parameterization, and hence so does a geodesic.






16.3.1.4 Definition: Complete length spaces: A length structure \((\mathcal{A}, L)\) on \(X\) is complete if for every \(p, q \in X\) there exists an admissible path \(\gamma\) joining them with \(L(\gamma) = d_L(p, q)\). Intrinsic metrics associated with complete length structures are said to be strictly intrinsic.


[点击展开翻译]

16.3.1.4 定义： 完备长度空间： \(X\) 上的一个长度结构 \((\mathcal{A}, L)\) 是完备的 (complete)，如果对于每一个 \(p, q \in X\)，都存在一条连接它们的容许路径 \(\gamma\)，满足 \(L(\gamma) = d_L(p, q)\)。与完备长度结构相关联的内蕴度量被称为严格内蕴的 (strictly intrinsic)。










16.3.1.5 Proposition: A length structure is complete iff there is a shortest path between every two points.


[点击展开翻译]

16.3.1.5 命题： 一个长度结构是完备的，当且仅当每两点之间都存在一条最短路径。




Intuitively, a space is noncomplete if a point is missing. One may suspect that this absence of a point can be noticed by moving along a geodesic that would end at this point: the interval for which our motion is well-defined is not closed. The theorem below formalizes this observation. Before formulating it, we generalize the notion of a shortest path so as to include paths defined on nonclosed intervals.






16.3.1.6 Definition: Minimal geodesics: A curve \(\gamma: I \to X\) in a metric space is a shortest path, or a minimal geodesic, if its restriction to any interval \([a, b] \subseteq I\) is a shortest path (Definition 16.3.1.1).


[点击展开翻译]

16.3.1.6 定义： 最小测地线： 度量空间中的曲线 \(\gamma: I \to X\) 是最短路径 (shortest path) 或最小测地线 (minimal geodesic)，如果它在任何区间 \([a, b] \subseteq I\) 上的限制都是一条最短路径（定义 16.3.1.1）。






16.3.2 The Hopf–Rinow theorem. | Hopf-Rinow 定理
The next theorem generalizes the classical Hopf–Rinow theorem for Riemannian manifolds.






16.3.2.1 Theorem: Hopf–Rinow–Cohn-Vossen: For a locally compact length space \((X, d)\), the following four assertions are equivalent:

\(X\) is complete.
\(X\) is boundedly compact, i.e., every closed metric ball in \(X\) is compact.
Every geodesic \(\gamma: [0, a[ \to X\) can be extended to a continuous path \(\overline{\gamma}: [0, a] \to X\).
There is a point \(p \in X\) such that every shortest path \(\gamma: [0, a[ \to X\) with \(\gamma(0) = p\) can be extended to a continuous path \(\overline{\gamma}: [0, a] \to X\).



[点击展开翻译]

16.3.2.1 定理： Hopf–Rinow–Cohn-Vossen： 对于局部紧的长度空间 \((X, d)\)，以下四个断言是等价的：

\(X\) 是完备的。
\(X\) 是有界紧的，即 \(X\) 中的每个闭度量球都是紧的。
每条测地线 \(\gamma: [0, a[ \to X\) 都可以扩张为连续路径 \(\overline{\gamma}: [0, a] \to X\)。
存在一点 \(p \in X\)，使得每条满足 \(\gamma(0) = p\) 的最短路径 \(\gamma: [0, a[ \to X\) 都可以扩张为连续路径 \(\overline{\gamma}: [0, a] \to X\)。







[点击展开证明]

Proof: Implications \((2) \implies (1) \implies (3) \implies (4)\) are left as easy exercises. We will prove that \((4)\) implies \((2)\).
Since \(X\) is locally compact, sufficiently small closed balls \(D_d(p, r)\) are compact. Assuming that there are noncompact closed balls for the sake of contradiction, assume that \[R = \sup\{r : D_d(p, r) \text{ is compact}\} < \infty. \qquad (248)\] The argument consists of two steps.
First, we prove that the open ball \(B_d(p, R)\) is pre-compact. It suffices to show that every sequence \(\{p_i\}\) in this ball contains a converging subsequence. Let \(r_i = d(p, p_i)\) for \(i \in \mathbb{N}\). One may assume that \(r_i \to R\) as \(i \to \infty\); otherwise a subsequence of \(\{p_i\}\) is contained in a ball \(D_d(p, r)\) for some \(r < R\), and then there is a converging subsequence because this smaller ball is compact by the choice of \(R\).
Let \(\gamma_i: [0, r_i] \to X\) be an arc-length parameterized shortest path connecting \(p\) to \(p_i\). Such a shortest path exists because \(p_i\) belongs to a compact ball centered at \(p\). We can choose a subsequence of \(\{\gamma_i\}\) such that the restrictions of the paths to \([0, r_1]\) converge. From this subsequence, we choose a further subsequence of paths whose restrictions to \([0, r_2]\) converge, and so on. Then the Cantor diagonal procedure yields a sequence \(\{\gamma_{i_n}\}\) (for all large enough \(n\)) such that for every \(t \in [0, R[\) the sequence \(\{\gamma_{i_n}(t)\}\) converges in \(X\).
Define \(\gamma(t) = \lim_{n \to \infty} \gamma_{i_n}(t)\); then \(\gamma: [0, R[ \to X\) is a nonexpanding map and a shortest path. By the assertion \((4)\), there is a continuous extension \(\overline{\gamma}: [0, R] \to X\). Then the points \(p_{i_n}\) converge to \(\overline{\gamma}(R)\).
Second, since the open ball \(B_d(p, R)\) is pre-compact, the closed ball \(D_d(p, R)\) in a length space, being the closure of the respective open ball, is compact. Now we show that a ball \(B_{R+\varepsilon}(p)\) is pre-compact for some \(\varepsilon > 0\). Since \(X\) is locally compact, for every \(q \in D_d(p, R)\) there is an \(r(q) > 0\) such that the ball \(B_d(q, r(q))\) is precompact. Choose \(\{q_i\}_{i=1}^N \subseteq D_d(p, R)\) so that \(D = \bigcup_{i=1}^N D_d(q_i, r(p_i)) \supseteq D_d(q, R)\). Since \(D\) is pre-compact and contains the ball \(B_d(p, R + \varepsilon)\) for \(\varepsilon = \min r_i > 0\), it contradicts the choice of \(R\). \(\blacksquare\)

title: COUNTABILITY
text:
Note that, a set \(X\) is countable if there is an injection \(X \to \mathbb{N}\); in particular, \(X\) may be finite or countably infinite.






14.1.0.1 Definition: The character \(\chi(p,\mathcal{T})\) of a topology \(\mathcal{T}\) on \(X\) at \(p \in X\) is the minimal cardinarity of neighborhood bases of \(\mathcal{T}\) at \(p\), and the character \(\chi(\mathcal{T})\) of \(\mathcal{T}\) is the supremum of \(\chi(p,\mathcal{T})\) over \(p \in X\). The weight \(w(\mathcal{T})\) of a topology \(\mathcal{T}\) is the minimal cardinarity of bases of \(\mathcal{T}\). The density \(d(\mathcal{T})\) of a topology \(\mathcal{T}\) on \(X\) is the minimal cardinarity of dense subsets of \((X,\mathcal{T})\).


[点击展开翻译]

14.1.0.1 定义： 拓扑空间 \(X\) 上拓扑 \(\mathcal{T}\) 在点 \(p \in X\) 处的特征 (character) \(\chi(p,\mathcal{T})\) 是在 \(p\) 处 \(\mathcal{T}\) 的邻域基的最小基数，而 \(\mathcal{T}\) 的特征 \(\chi(\mathcal{T})\) 是 \(\chi(p,\mathcal{T})\) 在所有 \(p \in X\) 上的上确界。拓扑 \(\mathcal{T}\) 的权重 (weight) \(w(\mathcal{T})\) 是 \(\mathcal{T}\) 的基的最小基数。拓扑空间 \((X,\mathcal{T})\) 的密度 (density) \(d(\mathcal{T})\) 是 \((X,\mathcal{T})\) 中稠密子集的最小基数。










14.1.0.2 Proposition: Let \(\mathcal{T}\) be a topology on \(X\). Then \[\chi(\mathcal{T}) \le w(\mathcal{T}) \ge d(\mathcal{T}). \qquad (224)\]


[点击展开翻译]

14.1.0.2 命题： 设 \(\mathcal{T}\) 是 \(X\) 上的一个拓扑。那么 \[\chi(\mathcal{T}) \le w(\mathcal{T}) \ge d(\mathcal{T}). \qquad (224)\]






[点击展开证明]

Proof: For any \(p \in X\), as \(\mathcal{N}_p \cap \mathcal{T} \subseteq \mathcal{T}\) we obtain that \(\chi(p,\mathcal{T}) \le w(\mathcal{T})\). Taking supermum over \(p\) we have \(\chi(\mathcal{T}) \le w(\mathcal{T})\). Give a base \(\mathcal{B}\) of \(\mathcal{T}\) we obtain a dense subset of \((X,\mathcal{T})\) by collecting a point in each of the member of \(\mathcal{B}\). Therefore, \(d(\mathcal{T}) \le w(\mathcal{T})\). \(\blacksquare\)







14.1.0.3 Definition: A topological space \((X,\mathcal{T})\) is

first countable iff it has countable character, or \(\chi(\mathcal{T}) \le |\mathbb{N}|\);
second countable iff it has countable weight, or \(w(\mathcal{T}) \le |\mathbb{N}|\);
separable iff it has countable density, or \(d(\mathcal{T}) \le |\mathbb{N}|\).



[点击展开翻译]

14.1.0.3 定义： 一个拓扑空间 \((X,\mathcal{T})\) 是

第一可数的 (first countable)，当且仅当它具有可数的特征，即 \(\chi(\mathcal{T}) \le |\mathbb{N}|\)；
第二可数的 (second countable)，当且仅当它具有可数的权重，即 \(w(\mathcal{T}) \le |\mathbb{N}|\)；
可分的 (separable)，当且仅当它具有可数的密度，即 \(d(\mathcal{T}) \le |\mathbb{N}|\)。













14.1.1.1 Theorem: A subspace of a separable space is separable.


[点击展开翻译]

14.1.1.1 定理： 可分空间的子空间是可分的。










14.1.1.2 Theorem: A countable product of separable spaces is separable.


[点击展开翻译]

14.1.1.2 定理： 可分空间的可数乘积是可分的。




14.1.1.3 Example: The product space \(\mathbb{R}^{[0,1]}\) is separable.






14.1.1.4 Proposition: If \(X\) is a separable topological space, then every collection of disjoint open subsets in \(X\) is countable.


[点击展开翻译]

14.1.1.4 命题： 如果 \(X\) 是一个可分的拓扑空间，那么 \(X\) 中任意互不相交的开子集族都是可数的。













[7, Theorem 30.2] 14.1.2.1 Theorem: A subspace of a first-countable space is first-countable.


[点击展开翻译]

[7, 定理 30.2] 14.1.2.1 定理： 第一可数空间的子空间是第一可数的。










[7, Theorem 30.2] 14.1.2.2 Theorem: A countable product of first-countable spaces is first-countable.


[点击展开翻译]

[7, 定理 30.2] 14.1.2.2 定理： 第一可数空间的可数乘积是第一可数的。










[7, Theorem 30.1] 14.1.2.3 Theorem: Let \(X\) be a first countable topological space. Then

it is Hausdorff iff every sequence in \(X\) has at most one limit point;
a point \(p \in X\) belongs to the closure of a subset \(A \subseteq X\) iff there is a sequence in \(A\) converging to \(p\);
a map \(f: X \to Y\) is continuous iff it sends convergent sequences to convergent sequences;
it is sequentially compact iff it is countably compact.



[点击展开翻译]

[7, 定理 30.1] 14.1.2.3 定理： 设 \(X\) 是一个第一可数拓扑空间。那么

它是 Hausdorff 空间，当且仅当 \(X\) 中的每个序列至多有一个极限点；
点 \(p \in X\) 属于子集 \(A \subseteq X\) 的闭包，当且仅当存在 \(A\) 中的序列收敛到 \(p\)；
映射 \(f: X \to Y\) 是连续的，当且仅当它将收敛序列映射为收敛序列；
它是序列紧的，当且仅当它是可数紧的。







[点击展开证明]

Proof: We prove the second item. Let \(\mathcal{B}_p = (U_n)_{n\in\mathbb{N}}\) be a countable neighborhood base of \(p \in X\). If \(p \in \overline{A}\) then there is \(p_n \in U_n \cap A\) for any \(n \in \mathbb{N}\). By definition of a neighborhood base, we have \(p_n \to p\). If there is \((p_k)_{k\in\mathbb{N}} \subseteq A\) with \(p_k \to p\), then for any \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\), by definition of a neighborhood base, there is \(U_n \subseteq U\) and then there is some \(p_k \in U_n \cap A \subseteq U \cap A\). Therefore, \(p \in \overline{A}\). \(\blacksquare\)










14.1.3.1 Theorem: Any second countable space is first countable, separable, and Lindelöf.


[点击展开翻译]

14.1.3.1 定理： 任何第二可数空间都是第一可数的、可分的且是 Lindelöf 空间。






[点击展开证明]

Proof: For the Lindelöfness, note that any open cover has a countable subcover by leaving only one member containing each basic open subset. \(\blacksquare\)

14.1.3.2 Corollary: Each base of a second countable space has a countable subbase.






14.1.3.3 Proposition: The lexicographical order space \(([0,1]^2, \mathcal{T}_{\text{lexi}})\) is separable but not first countable. The excluded point space \((X, \mathcal{T}_p')\) is first countable but not separable if \(X\) is uncountable.


[点击展开翻译]

14.1.3.3 命题： 字典序空间 \(([0,1]^2, \mathcal{T}_{\text{lexi}})\) 是可分的但不是第一可数的。排除点空间 \((X, \mathcal{T}_p')\) 是第一可数的，但如果 \(X\) 是不可数的，则它不是可分的。




14.1.3.4 Example: The long line is not second countable.
14.1.3.5 Example: The space \(\mathbb{R}_{\text{lower}}\) is first countable, separable, and Lindelöf, but not second countable.






14.1.3.6 Proposition: A locally compact, paracompact connected Hausdorff space is second-countable.


[点击展开翻译]

14.1.3.6 命题： 局部紧的、仿紧的连通 Hausdorff 空间是第二可数的。










[7, Theorem 30.2] 14.1.3.7 Theorem: A subspace of a second-countable space is second-countable.


[点击展开翻译]

[7, 定理 30.2] 14.1.3.7 定理： 第二可数空间的子空间是第二可数的。










[7, Theorem 30.2] 14.1.3.8 Theorem: A countable product of second-countable spaces is second-countable.


[点击展开翻译]

[7, 定理 30.2] 14.1.3.8 定理： 第二可数空间的可数乘积是第二可数的。






[点击展开证明]

Proof: Let \(X\) be a second-countable space with a countable base \(\mathcal{B}\). Then \(\{B \cap A : B \in \mathcal{B}\}\) is a countable base for the subspace \(A\) of \(X\). Let \(X_i\), \(i \in I\), be second-countable spaces with countable base \(\mathcal{B}_i\). Then \[\left\{ \prod_{i \in I} U_i : (\forall i \in I)(U_i \in \mathcal{B}_i) \wedge (U_i = X_i \text{ for all but finitely many } i \in I) \right\} \qquad (225)\] is a countable base for \(\prod_{i \in I} X_i\). \(\blacksquare\)

title: SEPARATION
text:
12.1 Separation axioms. | 分离公理。

12.1.1 Separating points and subsets. | 分离点与子集。
How points or closed sets are separated by opens sets?






12.1.1.1 Definition: Two points \(p\) and \(q\) of a topological space \(X\) are

distinct iff they are different, or \(p \neq q\);
topologically distinguishable iff there exists a neighborhood of one point away from the other point, or \((\exists U \in \mathcal{N}_p)(q \notin U) \lor (\exists V \in \mathcal{N}_q)(p \notin V)\).



[点击展开翻译]

12.1.1.1 定义：拓扑空间 \(X\) 中的两点 \(p\) 和 \(q\) 是

相异的 (distinct)，当且仅当它们不同，即 \(p \neq q\)；
拓扑可辨识的 (topologically distinguishable)，当且仅当存在一个点的一个邻域不包含另一个点，即 \((\exists U \in \mathcal{N}_p)(q \notin U) \lor (\exists V \in \mathcal{N}_q)(p \notin V)\)。











12.1.1.2 Definition: Let \(A\) be a subset of a topological space \(X\). analogous to those of a point, \(U\) is an open neighborhood of \(A\) in \(X\) iff \(U\) is open and contains \(A\), and \(U\) is a neighborhood of \(A\) in \(X\) iff it contains an open neighborhood of \(A\). The collection of neighborhoods of \(A\) in \(X\) is denoted by \(\mathcal{N}_A(X)\) or \(\mathcal{N}_A\).


[点击展开翻译]

12.1.1.2 定义：设 \(A\) 为拓扑空间 \(X\) 的一个子集。类似于点的邻域，当且仅当 \(U\) 是开集且包含 \(A\) 时，\(U\) 是 \(A\) 在 \(X\) 中的一个开邻域 (open neighborhood)；当且仅当 \(U\) 包含 \(A\) 的一个开邻域时，\(U\) 是 \(A\) 在 \(X\) 中的一个邻域 (neighborhood)。\(A\) 在 \(X\) 中的邻域族记作 \(\mathcal{N}_A(X)\) 或 \(\mathcal{N}_A\)。










12.1.1.3 Definition: Two subsets \(A\) and \(B\) of a topological space \(X\) are

disjoint iff their intersection is empty, or \(A \cap B = \emptyset\);
topologically disjoint iff there exists a neighborhood of one set that is disjoint from the other set, or \((\exists U \in \mathcal{N}_A)(U \cap B = \emptyset) \lor (\exists V \in \mathcal{N}_B)(V \cap A = \emptyset)\);
separated iff each set has a neighborhood that is disjoint from the other set, or \((\exists U \in \mathcal{N}_A)(\exists V \in \mathcal{N}_B)(U \cap B = V \cap A = \emptyset)\);
separated by neighborhoods iff they have disjoint neighborhoods, or \((\exists U \in \mathcal{N}_A)(\exists V \in \mathcal{N}_B)(U \cap V = \emptyset)\);
separated by closed neighborhoods iff they have disjoint closed neighborhoods, or \((\exists U \in \mathcal{N}_A)(\exists V \in \mathcal{N}_B)(\overline{U} \cap \overline{V} = \emptyset)\);
separated by a function iff there exists a continuous real-valued function on the space that maps \(A\) to 0 and \(B\) to 1, or \((\exists \text{ continuous } f: X \to \mathbb{R})(A \subseteq f^{-1}(0) \land B \subseteq f^{-1}(1))\); or
precisely separated by a function iff there exists a continuous real-valued function on the space that maps precisely \(A\) to 0 and \(B\) to 1, or \((\exists \text{ continuous } f: X \to \mathbb{R})(A = f^{-1}(0) \land B = f^{-1}(1))\).



[点击展开翻译]

12.1.1.3 定义：拓扑空间 \(X\) 中的两个子集 \(A\) 和 \(B\) 是

不相交的 (disjoint)，当且仅当它们的交集为空，即 \(A \cap B = \emptyset\)；
拓扑不相交的 (topologically disjoint)，当且仅当存在一个集合的一个邻域与另一个集合不相交，即 \((\exists U \in \mathcal{N}_A)(U \cap B = \emptyset) \lor (\exists V \in \mathcal{N}_B)(V \cap A = \emptyset)\)；
分离的 (separated)，当且仅当每个集合都有一个与另一个集合不相交的邻域，即 \((\exists U \in \mathcal{N}_A)(\exists V \in \mathcal{N}_B)(U \cap B = V \cap A = \emptyset)\)；
由邻域分离的 (separated by neighborhoods)，当且仅当它们有不相交的邻域，即 \((\exists U \in \mathcal{N}_A)(\exists V \in \mathcal{N}_B)(U \cap V = \emptyset)\)；
由闭邻域分离的 (separated by closed neighborhoods)，当且仅当它们有不相交的闭邻域，即 \((\exists U \in \mathcal{N}_A)(\exists V \in \mathcal{N}_B)(\overline{U} \cap \overline{V} = \emptyset)\)；
由函数分离的 (separated by a function)，当且仅当空间上存在一个连续实值函数将 \(A\) 映射为 0 并将 \(B\) 映射为 1，即 \((\exists \text{ continuous } f: X \to \mathbb{R})(A \subseteq f^{-1}(0) \land B \subseteq f^{-1}(1))\)；或
由函数精确分离的 (precisely separated by a function)，当且仅当空间上存在一个连续实值函数精确地将 \(A\) 映射为 0 并将 \(B\) 映射为 1，即 \((\exists \text{ continuous } f: X \to \mathbb{R})(A = f^{-1}(0) \land B = f^{-1}(1))\)。











12.1.1.4 Proposition: For two subsets of a topological space, being precisely separated by a function \(\implies\) separated by a function \(\implies\) separated by closed neighborhoods \(\implies\) separated by neighborhoods \(\implies\) separated \(\implies\) topologically disjoint \(\implies\) disjoint. For closed subsets, being separated \(\iff\) topologically disjoint \(\iff\) disjoint. For a closed set and a point, being topologically disjoint \(\iff\) disjoint. For two points, the root “tinct” means the same as “join”, and are called distinct iff they are disjoint, or called topologically distinguishable iff they are topologically disjoint.


[点击展开翻译]

12.1.1.4 命题：对于拓扑空间的两个子集，由函数精确分离 \(\implies\) 由函数分离 \(\implies\) 由闭邻域分离 \(\implies\) 由邻域分离 \(\implies\) 分离的 \(\implies\) 拓扑不相交的 \(\implies\) 不相交的。对于闭子集，分离的 \(\iff\) 拓扑不相交的 \(\iff\) 不相交的。对于一个闭集和一个点，拓扑不相交的 \(\iff\) 不相交的。对于两点，词根 “tinct” 的含义与 “join” 相同，当且仅当它们不相交时称为相异的 (distinct)，或者当且仅当它们拓扑不相交时称为拓扑可辨识的 (topologically distinguishable)。










12.1.1.5 Proposition: Two subsets \(A\) and \(B\) of a topological space \(X\) are separated iff they are disjoint from the closure of each other, in the sense of \(A \cap \overline{B} = \overline{A} \cap B = \emptyset\).\(^4\) Therefore, for two subsets, \[\text{separated by closed neighborhoods} \implies \text{separated by neighborhoods} \implies \text{separated,}\] \[\text{separated by closed neighborhoods} \implies \text{closures are disjoint} \implies \text{separated,} \quad (174)\] \[\text{disjoint from each other's closure} \iff \text{separated.}\] However, neither being “separated by neighborhoods” or “closures are disjoint” implies the other.


[点击展开翻译]

12.1.1.5 命题：拓扑空间 \(X\) 的两个子集 \(A\) 和 \(B\) 是分离的，当且仅当它们与彼此的闭包都不相交，即 \(A \cap \overline{B} = \overline{A} \cap B = \emptyset\)。\(^4\) 因此，对于两个子集， \[\text{由闭邻域分离} \implies \text{由邻域分离} \implies \text{分离的，}\] \[\text{由闭邻域分离} \implies \text{闭包不相交} \implies \text{分离的，} \quad (174)\] \[\text{与彼此的闭包不相交} \iff \text{分离的。}\] 然而，“由邻域分离”和“闭包不相交”两者均不能相互推导。




Proof: Let \(A = [0, 1[\) and \(B = ]1, 2]\) be subsets of \(\mathbb{R}\). They are separated by neighborhoods but closures are not disjoint. The other direction is an exercise. \(\blacksquare\)


12.1.2 Separation axioms. | 分离公理。






12.1.2.1 Definition: Let \(X\) be a topological space. We have the separation axioms.

\(\mathbf{T}_0\): any two distinct points in \(X\) are topologically distinguishable; or for any \(p,q \in X\), if \(p \neq q\) then either there is \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\) such that \(q \notin U\), or there is \(V \in \mathcal{N}_q\) such that \(p \notin V\);
\(\mathbf{T}_1\): any two distinct points in \(X\) are separated; or for any \(p,q \in X\), if \(p \neq q\) then there are \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\) and \(V \in \mathcal{N}_q\) such that \(p \notin V\) and \(q \notin U\);
\(\mathbf{T}_2\): any two distinct points in \(X\) are separated by neighborhoods; or for any \(p,q \in X\), if \(p \neq q\) then there are \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\) and \(V \in \mathcal{N}_q\) such that \(U \cap V = \emptyset\);
\(\mathbf{T}_{2\frac{1}{2}}\): any two distinct points in \(X\) are separated by closed neighborhoods; or for any \(p,q \in X\), if \(p \neq q\) then there are \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\) and \(V \in \mathcal{N}_q\) such that \(\overline{U} \cap \overline{V} = \emptyset\);
\(\mathbf{PT}_2\): any two distinct points in \(X\) are precisely separated by a function;
\(\mathbf{T}_3\): any disjoint point and closed set in \(X\) are separated by neighborhoods; or for any \(p \in X\) and closed \(B\) in \(X\), if \(p \notin B\) then there are \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\) and \(V \in \mathcal{N}_B\) such that \(U \cap V = \emptyset\);
\(\mathbf{T}_{3\frac{1}{2}}\): any disjoint point and closed set in \(X\) are separated by a function; or for any \(p \in X\) and closed \(B\) in \(X\), if \(p \notin B\) then there is a continuous function \(f: X \to \mathbb{R}\) with \(f(A) = 0\) and \(f(B) = 1\);
\(\mathbf{T}_4\): any disjoint closed sets in \(X\) are separated by neighborhoods; or for any closed \(A\) and \(B\) in \(X\), if \(A \cap B = \emptyset\) then there are \(U \in \mathcal{N}_A\) and \(V \in \mathcal{N}_B\) such that \(U \cap V = \emptyset\);
\(\mathbf{T}_5\): any separated sets in \(X\) are separated by neighborhoods; or for any \(A,B \subseteq X\), if \(\overline{A} \cap B = A \cap \overline{B} = \emptyset\) then there are \(U \in \mathcal{N}_A\) and \(V \in \mathcal{N}_B\) such that \(U \cap V = \emptyset\);
\(\mathbf{PT}_4\): any disjoint closed sets in \(X\) are precisely separated by a function.



[点击展开翻译]

12.1.2.1 定义：设 \(X\) 为一个拓扑空间。我们有以下分离公理：

\(\mathbf{T}_0\)：\(X\) 中任意两个相异点是拓扑可辨识的；或者对于任意 \(p,q \in X\)，如果 \(p \neq q\)，则要么存在 \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\) 使得 \(q \notin U\)，要么存在 \(V \in \mathcal{N}_q\) 使得 \(p \notin V\)；
\(\mathbf{T}_1\)：\(X\) 中任意两个相异点是分离的；或者对于任意 \(p,q \in X\)，如果 \(p \neq q\)，则存在 \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\) 和 \(V \in \mathcal{N}_q\) 使得 \(p \notin V\) 且 \(q \notin U\)；
\(\mathbf{T}_2\)：\(X\) 中任意两个相异点由邻域分离；或者对于任意 \(p,q \in X\)，如果 \(p \neq q\)，则存在 \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\) 和 \(V \in \mathcal{N}_q\) 使得 \(U \cap V = \emptyset\)；
\(\mathbf{T}_{2\frac{1}{2}}\)：\(X\) 中任意两个相异点由闭邻域分离；或者对于任意 \(p,q \in X\)，如果 \(p \neq q\)，则存在 \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\) 和 \(V \in \mathcal{N}_q\) 使得 \(\overline{U} \cap \overline{V} = \emptyset\)；
\(\mathbf{PT}_2\)：\(X\) 中任意两个相异点由函数精确分离；
\(\mathbf{T}_3\)：\(X\) 中任意不相交的点与闭集由邻域分离；或者对于任意 \(p \in X\) 以及 \(X\) 中的闭集 \(B\)，如果 \(p \notin B\)，则存在 \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\) 和 \(V \in \mathcal{N}_B\) 使得 \(U \cap V = \emptyset\)；
\(\mathbf{T}_{3\frac{1}{2}}\)：\(X\) 中任意不相交的点与闭集由函数分离；或者对于任意 \(p \in X\) 以及 \(X\) 中的闭集 \(B\)，如果 \(p \notin B\)，则存在连续函数 \(f: X \to \mathbb{R}\) 满足 \(f(A) = 0\) 和 \(f(B) = 1\)；
\(\mathbf{T}_4\)：\(X\) 中任意两个不相交的闭集由邻域分离；或者对于 \(X\) 中任意闭集 \(A\) 和 \(B\)，如果 \(A \cap B = \emptyset\)，则存在 \(U \in \mathcal{N}_A\) 和 \(V \in \mathcal{N}_B\) 使得 \(U \cap V = \emptyset\)；
\(\mathbf{T}_5\)：\(X\) 中任意分离集由邻域分离；或者对于任意 \(A,B \subseteq X\)，如果 \(\overline{A} \cap B = A \cap \overline{B} = \emptyset\)，则存在 \(U \in \mathcal{N}_A\) 和 \(V \in \mathcal{N}_B\) 使得 \(U \cap V = \emptyset\)；
\(\mathbf{PT}_4\)：\(X\) 中任意不相交的闭集由函数精确分离。











12.1.2.2 Definition: A topological space \(X\) is called

a \(\mathbf{T}_0\) space, or a Kolmogorov space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_0\) axiom;
a \(\mathbf{T}_1\) space, or a Fréchet space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_1\) axiom;
a \(\mathbf{T}_2\) space, or a Hausdorff space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_2\) axiom;
a \(\mathbf{T}_{2\frac{1}{2}}\) space, or a completely Hausdorff space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_{2\frac{1}{2}}\) axiom;
a perfectly \(\mathbf{T}_2\) space, or a perfectly Hausdorff space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{PT}_2\) axiom;
a \(\mathbf{T}_3\) space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_3\) axiom;
a regular space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_0\) and \(\mathbf{T}_3\) axioms;
a \(\mathbf{T}_{3\frac{1}{2}}\) space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_{3\frac{1}{2}}\) axiom;
a completely regular space, or a Tychonoff space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_0\) and \(\mathbf{T}_{3\frac{1}{2}}\) axioms;
a \(\mathbf{T}_4\) space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_4\) axiom;
a normal space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_1\) and \(\mathbf{T}_4\) axioms;
a \(\mathbf{T}_5\) space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_5\) axiom.
a completely normal space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{T}_1\) and \(\mathbf{T}_5\) axioms;
a perfectly \(\mathbf{T}_4\) space, iff it satisfies the \(\mathbf{PT}_4\) axiom.



[点击展开翻译]

12.1.2.2 定义：一个拓扑空间 \(X\) 被称为

\(\mathbf{T}_0\) 空间，或 柯尔莫哥洛夫空间 (Kolmogorov space)，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_0\) 公理；
\(\mathbf{T}_1\) 空间，或 弗雷歇空间 (Fréchet space)，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_1\) 公理；
\(\mathbf{T}_2\) 空间，或 豪斯多夫空间 (Hausdorff space)，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_2\) 公理；
\(\mathbf{T}_{2\frac{1}{2}}\) 空间，或 完全豪斯多夫空间 (completely Hausdorff space)，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_{2\frac{1}{2}}\) 公理；
完美 \(\mathbf{T}_2\) 空间 (perfectly \(\mathbf{T}_2\) space)，或 完美豪斯多夫空间 (perfectly Hausdorff space)，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{PT}_2\) 公理；
\(\mathbf{T}_3\) 空间，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_3\) 公理；
正则空间 (regular space)，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_0\) 和 \(\mathbf{T}_3\) 公理；
\(\mathbf{T}_{3\frac{1}{2}}\) 空间，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_{3\frac{1}{2}}\) 公理；
完全正则空间 (completely regular space)，或 吉洪诺夫空间 (Tychonoff space)，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_0\) 和 \(\mathbf{T}_{3\frac{1}{2}}\) 公理；
\(\mathbf{T}_4\) 空间，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_4\) 公理；
正规空间 (normal space)，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_1\) 和 \(\mathbf{T}_4\) 公理；
\(\mathbf{T}_5\) 空间，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_5\) 公理；
完全正规空间 (completely normal space)，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{T}_1\) 和 \(\mathbf{T}_5\) 公理；
完美 \(\mathbf{T}_4\) 空间 (perfectly \(\mathbf{T}_4\) space)，当且仅当它满足 \(\mathbf{PT}_4\) 公理。







[点击展开证明]

Proof: The first item is rewriting topological distinguishability. The embedding \(\iota\) exists with \(\iota(0) = p\) and \(\iota(1) = q\), \(p \neq q\) iff any open \(U\) we have \(U \cap \{p, q\}\) is either \(\emptyset\) or \(\{p, q\}\), which is equivalent to \(X\) not being \(\mathbf{T}_0\). \(\blacksquare\)
The following chart summarizes the various separation axioms we mentioned above.

where the headline abbreviates topologically disjoint, separated, separated by neighborhoods, separated by closed neighborhoods, separated by a function, and precisely separated by a function, respectively.



12.1.3 Implication of separation axioms.






12.1.3.1 Remark: A higher \(\mathbf{T}_i\) axiom does not necessarily imply a lower one.


[点击展开翻译]

12.1.3.1 备注：较高的 \(\mathbf{T}_i\) 公理不一定蕴含较低的公理。










12.1.3.2 Proposition: \[
\begin{aligned}
\mathbf{T}_5 &\implies \mathbf{T}_4 \\
\mathbf{T}_4 \land \mathbf{T}_3 &\implies \mathbf{T}_{3\frac{1}{2}} \\
\mathbf{T}_4 \land \mathbf{T}_1 &\implies \mathbf{T}_{3\frac{1}{2}} \implies \mathbf{T}_3 \\
\mathbf{T}_3 \land \mathbf{T}_2 &\implies \mathbf{T}_{2\frac{1}{2}} \\
\mathbf{T}_3 \land \mathbf{T}_0 &\implies \mathbf{T}_{2\frac{1}{2}} \implies \mathbf{T}_2 \implies \mathbf{T}_1 \implies \mathbf{T}_0
\end{aligned} \qquad (175)
\]





[点击展开证明]

Proof: \(\mathbf{T}_5 \implies \mathbf{T}_4\). Any two disjoint closed sets are separated. \(\mathbf{T}_3 \land \mathbf{T}_0 \implies \mathbf{T}_2\). Let \(p, q \in X\) be two distinct points. By \(\mathbf{T}_0\), there is \(U \in \mathcal{T}\) containing exactly one of \(p\) and \(q\), and we assume without loss of generality that \(q \in U\) but \(p \notin U\). By \(\mathbf{T}_3\), there is \(V \in \mathcal{N}_q\) and \(W \in \mathcal{N}_{X \setminus U}\) with \(W \cap V = \emptyset\). In particular, \(W \in \mathcal{N}_p\) and \(X\) satisfies \(\mathbf{T}_2\). \(\mathbf{T}_3 \land \mathbf{T}_2 \implies \mathbf{T}_{2\frac{1}{2}}\). Let \(p, q \in X\) be two distinct points. By \(\mathbf{T}_2\), there are \(U \in \mathcal{N}_p\) and \(V \in \mathcal{N}_q\) such that \(U \cap V = \emptyset\). By \(\mathbf{T}_3\), there is \(U_1 \in \mathcal{N}_p\) and \(V_1 \in \mathcal{N}_q\) such that \(\overline{U_1} \subseteq U\) and \(\overline{V_1} \subseteq V\). Then \(\overline{U_1} \cap \overline{V_1} \subseteq U \cap V = \emptyset\) and \(X\) satisfies \(\mathbf{T}_{2\frac{1}{2}}\). \(\mathbf{T}_{2\frac{1}{2}} \implies \mathbf{T}_2 \implies \mathbf{T}_1 \implies \mathbf{T}_0\). Being separated by neighborhoods \(\implies\) separated \(\implies\) topologically disjoint. \(\blacksquare\)
The following chart summarizes examples of spaces which satisfies certain \(\mathbf{T}_i\) axioms.

\(\mathbf{T}_0 \land \neg\mathbf{T}_1\)
\(\mathbf{T}_1 \land \neg\mathbf{T}_2\)

title: SEPARATION
section: 12.2 Kolmogorov spaces. | 柯尔莫哥洛夫空间。
text:
12.2 Kolmogorov spaces. | 柯尔莫哥洛夫空间。

12.2.1 Kolmogorov spaces. | 柯尔莫哥洛夫空间。






12.2.1.1 Proposition: A topological space \(X\) is \(\mathrm{T}_0\)

iff any two distinct points in \(X\) has different neighborhoods;
iff there is no continuous injection \(f: (\{0, 1\}, \mathcal{T}_{\text{triv}}) \to X\).



[点击展开翻译]

12.2.1.1 命题：一个拓扑空间 \(X\) 是 \(\mathrm{T}_0\) 空间

当且仅当 \(X\) 中任意两个相异点有不同的邻域；
当且仅当不存在连续单射 \(f: (\{0, 1\}, \mathcal{T}_{\text{triv}}) \to X\)。











12.2.1.2 Proposition: The topological indistinguishablility is an equivalence relation on the topological space.


[点击展开翻译]

12.2.1.2 命题：拓扑不可辨识性是拓扑空间上的一个等价关系。










12.2.1.3 Proposition: Let \(p_0, p_1 \in X\) be topologically indistinguishable, then the bijection \(f: X \to X\) swapping \(p_0\) and \(p_1\) is a homeomorphism.


[点击展开翻译]

12.2.1.3 命题：设 \(p_0, p_1 \in X\) 是拓扑不可辨识的，则交换 \(p_0\) 和 \(p_1\) 的双射 \(f: X \to X\) 是一个同胚。










Example Sierpiński 空间
集合 \(X = \{0, 1\}\)，拓扑为 \(\tau = \{\emptyset, \{1\}, \{0, 1\}\}\)。对于点 0 和 1，开集 \(\{1\}\) 包含 1 但不包含 0，满足 \(T_0\)





12.2.2 Kolmogorov quotients. | 柯尔莫哥洛夫商。






12.2.2.1 Definition: The quotient of a topological space \(X\) under topological indistinguishablility is called its Kolmogorov quotient.


[点击展开翻译]

12.2.2.1 定义：拓扑空间 \(X\) 在拓扑不可辨识性下的商称为其柯尔莫哥洛夫商 (Kolmogorov quotient)。
